comments.message: Depois de mudar o alias da conta, a página de integrações mostra conectores desconectados, embora os dados ainda cheguem pela API. Ao abrir detalhes, dá erro inesperado.
Se os dados continuam chegando, então está tudo normal. O status da página pode demorar para atualizar depois de mudanças administrativas.
Mas já se passaram 3 dias e os detalhes da integração continuam quebrados. Parece mais do que atraso de status.
Entendi, mas no momento não vejo impacto funcional crítico. Sugiro ignorar o aviso por enquanto e monitorar. Se a API parar de enviar dados, entre em contato novamente.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Relatório por equipe não reflete mudança de gestor dos usuários
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Reorganizamos as equipes e movi 23 usuários de um gestor para outro pelo painel de administração. A alteração já aparece nos perfis, mas o relatório por equipe continua atribuindo esses usuários ao gestor antigo. Isso está gerando cobrança errada entre áreas. Preciso saber se o relatório usa outro campo além do gestor atual.

comments.message: A mudança foi feita ontem às 16h. Nos perfis dos usuários já consta a nova gestora, mas no relatório exportado hoje cedo ainda veio tudo no gestor antigo.

Olá! Esse relatório usa um snapshot hierárquico diário para manter consistência histórica. Alterações de gestor feitas no módulo de administração depois do fechamento diário só entram no próximo ciclo de atualização, normalmente durante a madrugada.
Entendi. Mas eu preciso corrigir o arquivo de hoje, não o de amanhã.

Para o arquivo de hoje, a alternativa é gerar um relatório personalizado baseado em 'gestor atual do usuário' em vez de 'hierarquia no período'. Esse campo lê o cadastro mais recente. Se preferir, posso orientar como duplicar o relatório atual e trocar essa dimensão.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Former employee still listed as security contact after admin change
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: We deactivated our old IT manager last week and promoted a new one, but I just noticed security notifications are still addressed to the former employee. I need help understanding which contact records live outside user management and how to fully update them. If this can't be done in-app, please route it to the right team.

comments.message: I assumed deactivating the old IT manager would remove them everywhere. Instead, they're still listed on security emails and maybe compliance notices too. Where do I change that?

Deactivating a user only removes their login access and assigned role; it doesn't always update separate account contacts like security, billing, or legal notifications. Those fields may live under account profile or subscription records rather than user management.
I can't find any security contact field in our admin pages. Is it hidden by plan?

It may not be self-service on your account, especially if those contacts were set during onboarding or on an enterprise agreement. Please send the replacement contact's full name, company email, and whether they should receive security-only notices or all compliance communications. I'm escalating this to our account operations team to update the non-user contact records and confirm whether any of those settings can be exposed to your admins going forward.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Importação via API criou caracteres quebrados e colunas desalinhadas
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Fizemos uma importação para popular registros a partir de um CSV processado no nosso middleware e enviado pela API. Depois da carga, vários nomes com acento ficaram corrompidos e alguns valores foram parar em campos errados, principalmente quando havia quebra de linha em observações. Agora precisamos corrigir os dados já gravados e entender qual serialização a API espera para não repetir o problema.

comments.message: O CSV original está em UTF-8 e no nosso lado parece normal. O problema aparece só depois da criação dos registros na plataforma.

Olá! Se a importação foi feita pela API, o ideal é não enviar CSV bruto, mas objetos JSON já parseados e validados. Caracteres corrompidos normalmente indicam problema de encoding no middleware, e colunas desalinhadas podem acontecer se as quebras de linha não forem escapadas corretamente antes da transformação.
Sim, transformamos o CSV em JSON antes de enviar. Existe alguma forma de reverter em massa os registros criados ontem à tarde?

Você pode excluir os registros afetados e reenviar a importação depois de corrigir o parser. Para identificar os itens, filtre por created_at na janela de ontem e exporte os campos impactados. Se não for viável excluir, faça um update em massa com os valores corretos após regenerar o JSON usando UTF-8 normalizado e aspas escapadas nas observações.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Sincronização incremental perdeu atualizações após mudança de fuso
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Nosso job incremental usa o campo updated_at da API para carregar alterações em contas e negócios para o banco analítico. Depois da mudança de fuso do nosso servidor, percebemos que várias atualizações de uma janela de cerca de 1 hora não entraram no pipeline. Precisamos recuperar esses dados sem duplicar o restante e confirmar se a API sempre retorna timestamps em UTC ou se há alguma dependência de configuração da conta.

comments.message: Guardávamos o último horário processado em horário local e convertíamos antes da chamada. Acho que aí começou o problema.

Olá! Os timestamps da API são retornados em UTC, então o ideal é armazenar e comparar sempre em UTC no seu pipeline. Para recuperar a lacuna, reexecute a carga desde antes da janela afetada usando uma margem de segurança e faça upsert por id no banco de destino.
Perfeito. Existe risco de perder updates com o mesmo segundo exato do checkpoint?

Sim, se você usar um corte estritamente maior que o último timestamp processado. Para evitar isso, reutilize o último checkpoint com overlap de alguns minutos e deduplique por id + updated_at no destino. Se preferir, posso também compartilhar um exemplo de estratégia de watermark para evitar esse tipo de falha em cargas incrementais.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Não consigo dar acesso ao relatório personalizado para novos gestores
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Sou administradora da conta e criei dois novos gestores hoje. Eles conseguem entrar no sistema, mas não conseguem ver um relatório personalizado de performance que já existe há meses. Eu já atribuí o papel de gerente e coloquei os dois no mesmo grupo dos outros gestores que veem esse relatório. Preciso liberar isso ainda hoje.

comments.message: Os usuários são a Renata e o Caio. Ambos aparecem como ativos e no grupo 'Gestores Regionais'. Mesmo assim, ao abrir Relatórios, eles só veem os relatórios padrão.

Olá! Obrigado pelas informações. Além do papel e do grupo, relatórios personalizados podem estar restritos ao proprietário original ou a uma lista de compartilhamento. Você pode abrir o relatório, clicar em 'Compartilhar' e confirmar se ele está definido para 'Todos no grupo Gestores Regionais'?
Fui verificar e estava compartilhado só com usuários específicos antigos. Mudei para o grupo. A Renata já consegue ver, mas o Caio ainda não.

Ótimo avanço. Se apenas o Caio ainda não vê, provavelmente o cache de permissões dele ainda não atualizou. Peça para ele sair e entrar novamente ou aguardar alguns minutos. Se persistir após 15 minutos, me envie o e-mail dele para eu validar a associação de grupo no backend.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: HubSpot install finished, but custom lifecycle field won't map
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: I'm trying to connect HubSpot and use one of our custom lifecycle fields inside your scoring workflow. The install succeeded and standard fields are syncing, but our custom property doesn't appear in the mapping dropdown. I need to know if I'm missing a setup step or if there are limits on which fields can be used.

comments.message: The HubSpot integration is active and I can see contacts syncing, but a custom property we rely on for lifecycle stage is missing from your field mapping list. How do I make it available?

Custom HubSpot properties should show up automatically after the initial metadata sync, so first click "Refresh schema" in Integrations > HubSpot. Also check that the property is created on the same object type you're mapping—contact properties won't appear in company mappings, for example.
I refreshed the schema twice. The property is on contacts, and I'm editing a contact mapping. Still not there.

In that case, please verify the HubSpot property type. Our scoring workflows only support text, number, date, and single-select fields for mapping; calculation and multi-checkbox properties are excluded from the dropdown. If your lifecycle field is one of those unsupported types, the best workaround is to mirror it into a supported custom property in HubSpot and sync that field instead.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Exportação de relatório inclui usuários desativados
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Desativei alguns colaboradores antigos no módulo de administração de usuários, mas eles continuam aparecendo na exportação CSV do relatório de utilização. Na tela do relatório eu marco para ver somente usuários ativos, porém no arquivo exportado os desativados ainda vêm juntos. Isso está confundindo o financeiro aqui.

comments.message: Os usuários foram desativados no começo do mês. Na visualização da tela parece certo, mas quando baixo o CSV aparecem nomes de pessoas desligadas com status vazio.

Olá! Entendi o problema. O filtro de 'ativos' na tela nem sempre é aplicado na exportação quando o relatório usa um modelo salvo antigo. Você consegue me dizer se está abrindo um relatório salvo antes de desativar esses usuários?
Sim, é um relatório salvo que usamos todo mês. Mas mesmo duplicando o modelo o CSV continua trazendo os desativados.
Obrigado por testar. Nesse caso, pode ser uma limitação da exportação do relatório legado. Como alternativa, sugiro excluir os usuários permanentemente em Administração para que não apareçam mais.
Excluir permanentemente não é opção por auditoria. Precisamos manter histórico.

Entendo. Então vou encaminhar para o time responsável por relatórios porque a desativação deveria respeitar o filtro de exportação sem exigir exclusão. Enquanto isso, a única saída prática é filtrar manualmente a coluna de status no CSV.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Gráfico por agente fica em branco ao aplicar comparação com período anterior
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Quando eu abro um relatório personalizado de desempenho por agente e ativo a opção de comparar com o período anterior, o gráfico some completamente e aparece só o eixo vazio. Sem comparação ele carrega normal. Isso começou ontem e está acontecendo com outros gestores aqui também. Quero saber se é um erro conhecido e se existe alguma forma de recuperar a visualização.

comments.message: O relatório afetado é um gráfico de barras com atendimentos resolvidos por agente. Se eu marco “comparar com período anterior”, a página fica carregando e depois aparece o gráfico em branco, sem barras nenhuma.
Olá! Obrigado por reportar. Esse tipo de comportamento geralmente está ligado ao navegador. Poderia testar em outro browser e desabilitar extensões, principalmente bloqueadores?
Já testei no Chrome e no Edge, com extensões desativadas. Também pedi para outra pessoa do time abrir e aconteceu igual. No console aparece erro 500 numa chamada para /reports/compare.

Entendi. Nesse caso, não parece ser algo local. Fiz um teste rápido aqui e realmente a rota de comparação está falhando para alguns relatórios agregados por agente. Vou escalar para o time responsável por Relatórios com a evidência do erro 500. Enquanto isso, a única alternativa é duplicar o relatório e abrir dois períodos lado a lado, porque a comparação embutida está indisponível no momento.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Cursor da API reinicia e repete páginas no meio da sincronização
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Estamos migrando nossa replicação de offset para cursor, mas em um volume alto de tickets o next_cursor às vezes volta para um valor já usado e a sincronização entra em loop, repetindo páginas e enchendo nossa área de staging com duplicados. Precisamos saber se isso é esperado em datasets mutáveis e qual controle de estado deveríamos adotar para manter a consistência dos dados sem interromper a integração.

comments.message: Percebemos isso principalmente quando novos tickets entram durante a leitura. Guardamos o cursor em Redis e retomamos a cada página.

Olá! O cursor não deveria voltar para um valor anterior em condições normais. Você consegue compartilhar um exemplo de resposta com o cursor repetido e o horário aproximado? Com isso consigo verificar se houve instabilidade no serviço de paginação.
Posso compartilhar, mas preciso de orientação imediata porque o processo está pausado. O que fazemos para não duplicar tudo enquanto investigamos?

Como mitigação imediata, recomendo manter um conjunto de IDs já processados por execução e gravar a última combinação estável de cursor + primeiro/último ID da página. Se detectar repetição do mesmo cursor com a mesma faixa de IDs, interrompa o ciclo e reinicie a leitura a partir de uma janela temporal menor, aplicando merge por id no destino. Vou abrir uma investigação com nosso time de API para validar esse comportamento no seu tenant.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Need escalation for duplicate tenant creation
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: During implementation, your provisioning flow appears to have created two tenants for our company with slightly different names. Our users are split across both, and some integrations point to one tenant while SSO points to the other. We can't clean this up ourselves from the admin side. Please escalate to whoever can merge or decommission the wrong tenant without data loss.

comments.message: This is causing real confusion for our rollout. We need a safe path to consolidate the environments.

I reviewed the account and I can indeed see two active tenants tied to your organization domain. Tenant merges or reassignments aren't something support can perform because they involve data integrity checks and backend operations.
Okay, but we need guidance on what happens first so nobody loses access.

I've escalated the case to our Provisioning team and flagged the risk of data loss and SSO disruption. They will need to assess which tenant should remain primary before any merge or decommission step. Until they respond, please avoid changing SSO settings or deleting users in either tenant so they have a stable state to review.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Can't use magic link login and Marketo sync stopped matching leads
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Our contractors use magic link login because they are outside our SSO directory. Since yesterday, magic links are failing for that group after we tightened domain access rules. At the same time, our Marketo sync began inserting duplicate leads instead of updating existing ones, and source campaign values are blank in newly imported records. I need to get people back into the app and stop the bad data from piling up.

comments.message: We restricted access to approved domains and turned on SSO enforcement for employees. Contractors are supposed to stay on magic links, but now they get 'access method not allowed.' Separately, the Marketo connector is creating duplicate leads and source campaign is empty on all records since this morning.

For the login issue, it sounds like the domain restriction policy is catching contractor emails before the magic-link exception is applied. Add their domains to the allowed list or create an access policy group that permits passwordless login. On Marketo, duplicates plus blank source campaign usually mean the sync user lost visibility to the dedupe key and campaign field. Did the Marketo connection use one of the employee accounts now forced onto SSO?
Yes, the Marketo connection was under a former sales ops employee account that is now suspended. We can add the contractor domains, but we need the import fixed too.

Understood. Reconnect Marketo using a non-human service account that is excluded from SSO and has access to the lead external key plus source campaign field. Once reconnected, run a dry-run sync first to confirm updates are matching instead of creating new leads. That should stop new duplicates, but the duplicate leads already created will need separate cleanup.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Comparativo trimestral soma janeiro duas vezes no dashboard
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Meu dashboard executivo com relatório comparativo trimestral está somando janeiro em duplicidade no cartão de receitas por cliente. Fevereiro e março parecem normais, mas o total do trimestre fica inflado exatamente pelo valor de janeiro repetido. Já revisei os filtros, a fórmula calculada e o período, e não encontrei erro de configuração. Preciso saber se vocês conseguem investigar isso com urgência.

comments.message: O widget afetado é o cartão “Receita líquida por cliente - trimestre”. Quando abro o detalhamento por mês, janeiro aparece duas vezes na composição do total trimestral, embora a tabela abaixo liste janeiro só uma vez.
Olá! Talvez exista um problema na sua fórmula calculada, especialmente se estiver usando soma acumulada trimestral. Você pode recriar a métrica em vez de reutilizar a existente?
Eu já recriei a métrica do zero e o resultado continua igual. Inclusive outro dashboard usando a mesma fonte apresenta a mesma duplicidade só no cartão, não na tabela.

Entendi. Nesse cenário, é mais provável que o bug esteja na renderização/agregação do componente de cartão para comparativos trimestrais, e não na sua fórmula. Vou abrir um incidente com o time de produto de Relatórios e anexar que a tabela base não duplica janeiro, apenas o cartão resumido. Se puder, mantenha a tabela como fonte de verdade até corrigirmos o widget.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Criação em massa de usuários falhou e o relatório ficou duplicado
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Fiz uma importação em massa de usuários por CSV ontem à noite. Alguns usuários foram criados duas vezes com e-mails alternativos e agora o relatório de adoção mostra 128 contas, quando deveriam ser 121. Eu já desativei os duplicados, mas o relatório continua contando todos. Como corrigir isso sem perder o histórico dos usuários válidos?

comments.message: Os duplicados foram gerados porque a planilha tinha uma coluna de e-mail secundário e o importador aparentemente aceitou como novo login. Hoje cedo desativei 7 contas repetidas.

Olá! Obrigado pelo contexto. Quando usuários duplicados são desativados depois de terem sido sincronizados com o relatório de adoção, a contagem total pode continuar considerando registros históricos até a próxima recomputação.
Existe alguma forma de forçar essa recomputação? Preciso do número correto para apresentar amanhã.

Sim. Vá em Relatórios > Adoção > Configurações e clique em 'Reconstruir métricas'. Isso recalcula usuários ativos e exclui desativados da contagem atual, mantendo o histórico de eventos. O processo pode levar até 2 horas. Se quiser uma solução mais rápida para amanhã, filtre a métrica por 'Status do usuário = Ativo' no período atual.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Anexos baixados pela API sem vínculo confiável com os registros
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Estamos extraindo anexos pela API para um repositório central, mas o endpoint de arquivos nem sempre traz de forma clara a qual ticket ou empresa cada arquivo pertence. Em alguns casos só temos o file_id e o nome do arquivo, o que dificulta a governança e a retenção correta dos dados. Precisamos montar um inventário consistente e entender qual relacionamento deve ser usado para mapear os anexos ao objeto de origem.

comments.message: Nossa auditoria pediu rastreabilidade completa de anexos para titularidade e retenção. Sem esse vínculo, o processo fica incompleto.

Olá! Normalmente o relacionamento vem pelo endpoint do objeto de origem, não pelo endpoint de arquivos. A prática recomendada é listar os anexos a partir de tickets, comentários ou registros pai, e não tratar o arquivo como entidade principal para reconciliação.
Mas já temos milhares de arquivos históricos baixados apenas pelo endpoint de arquivos. Como reconstruímos o vínculo agora?

Para o histórico, você pode cruzar pelo nome do arquivo e pela data de criação com os comentários e registros que contenham anexos no mesmo período. Não é perfeito, mas costuma resolver a maioria dos casos.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Relatório de atividade não mostra novos usuários da filial
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Eu administro os usuários da nossa conta e criei 14 pessoas novas para a filial de Curitiba na semana passada. Elas já conseguiram entrar na plataforma, mas quando puxo o relatório de atividade por departamento esses usuários não aparecem de jeito nenhum. Já revisei os filtros de data e de equipe. Preciso fechar um acompanhamento hoje e os números estão errados.

comments.message: Criei os usuários pelo painel de administração na terça e na quarta. Todos receberam convite e acessaram pelo menos uma vez. No relatório de atividade por departamento, a filial de Curitiba continua com o mesmo total antigo.

Olá! Obrigado por detalhar. Isso normalmente acontece quando os usuários são criados sem um departamento principal definido no perfil. Pode abrir Administração > Usuários, editar um dos usuários afetados e confirmar se o campo 'Departamento' está preenchido como 'Curitiba'? Depois disso, o relatório costuma atualizar em até 30 minutos.

Verifiquei agora. Em vários deles o departamento estava em branco, apesar de eu ter colocado o grupo correto na criação em massa. Corrigi 3 perfis e eles já apareceram no relatório, mas os outros 11 ainda não.

Perfeito, então identificamos a causa. O grupo de acesso não preenche automaticamente o campo de departamento no relatório. Recomendo atualizar os 11 perfis restantes ou usar a edição em massa para definir 'Departamento = Curitiba'. Após a sincronização, o relatório deve fechar corretamente. Se quiser, posso te passar o passo a passo da edição em massa.

subject: Offboarded user keeps getting reassigned open tasks

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Round-robin assignment broke after adding a new queue
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: I added a new SMB queue and updated our round-robin automation so incoming chats should assign to the next available agent within the selected queue. Since that change, chats in the Enterprise queue are all going to the same rep while SMB seems fine. I may have changed something in the queue configuration that affected the shared assignment workflow.

comments.message: Before adding SMB, Enterprise round-robin was balanced. Now every Enterprise chat is assigned to Dana. The workflow chooses queue based on customer segment, then uses round-robin assign.

It sounds like the queue order reset. Please go to Product Configuration > Queues and make sure all Enterprise agents are active and eligible for chat, then inspect the round-robin node in the workflow. If the assignment action is set to "last assigned in selected pool," adding a new queue can sometimes rebuild the pool membership. Re-saving the Enterprise queue membership usually fixes it.
All Enterprise agents are active. I re-saved membership and tested three chats; they still all went to Dana.

Thanks for checking. I’m not confident this is a simple config issue anymore. There may be a defect in how the round-robin cursor was recalculated after the new queue was added. I’ve escalated this to our automation team with your findings; in the meantime, a temporary workaround is to switch Enterprise to load-balanced assignment or recreate the Enterprise queue and point the workflow to the new queue ID.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Audit report shows disabled users as active managers
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: I was reviewing our quarterly user administration audit and noticed that two disabled users still appear as active managers in a permissions report. When I click into their profiles they are clearly deactivated, but the exported report is wrong and leadership is asking whether our access review can be trusted. One of those users also used to own a compliance report schedule, so I’m not sure if stale reporting data is tied to the account change history.

comments.message: I was reviewing our quarterly user administration audit and noticed that two disabled users still appear as active managers in a permissions report. When I click into their profiles they are clearly deactivated, but the exported report is wrong and leadership is asking whether our access review can be trusted. One of those users also used to own a compliance report schedule, so I’m not sure if stale reporting data is tied to the account change history.

This can happen when the user status field in reporting lags behind account updates or when the report is based on historical role snapshots rather than current user state. First, check the report definition to see whether it uses “Current Status” or an audit/event dataset. If it’s tied to a historical dataset, disabled users can still appear as active for the time period covered.
The export is supposed to be current-state only, and it was run today. We disabled those users last month.

Got it. That suggests either a stale reporting cache or a mismatch between the user directory table and the permissions-report dataset. I can’t verify the backend from here, so I’m escalating this to engineering with your export timestamp and the affected user IDs. For now, I’d avoid using that specific permissions export for the audit and rely on the user directory view as the source of truth until we confirm the reporting sync status.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Usunięcie byłego pracownika z konta nie zwolniło miejsca
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Wczoraj dezaktywowałem pracownika, który odszedł z firmy, bo chcemy dodać nową osobę bez kupowania kolejnej licencji. System nadal pokazuje brak wolnych miejsc. Nie wiem, czy trzeba coś jeszcze zmienić w koncie.

comments.message: Wyłączyłem konto użytkownika m.kulesza@firma.pl, ale przy dodawaniu nowej osoby nadal mam komunikat, że wykorzystano wszystkie licencje. Czy dezaktywacja nie zwalnia miejsca?
Dzień dobry, zależy od typu planu. W większości planów samo wyłączenie użytkownika zwalnia miejsce dopiero na początku kolejnego cyklu rozliczeniowego.
To trochę mylące, bo w panelu jest napisane tylko „użytkownik nieaktywny”. Potrzebuję dodać nową osobę jeszcze dziś.

Rozumiem. Sprawdziłem konto i u Państwa licencje są rozliczane według maksymalnej liczby aktywnych stanowisk w cyklu, więc miejsce technicznie może być zajęte do końca miesiąca. Mogę jednak tymczasowo dodać jednego użytkownika testowego na 7 dni bez naliczania dodatkowej opłaty i równolegle zasugerować zmianę na model z elastycznymi miejscami. Czy mam to uruchomić?

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Former employee still has access and our termination workflow didn't run
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: We offboarded an employee yesterday, but she was still able to log in this morning through Google SSO. Also, the offboarding workflow that should revoke assigned approvals and transfer ownership never ran for her open requests. This is a security issue, so if you can't fix it directly please escalate it to the right team right away.

comments.message: We offboarded an employee yesterday, but she was still able to log in this morning through Google SSO. Also, the offboarding workflow that should revoke assigned approvals and transfer ownership never ran for her open requests. This is a security issue, so if you can't fix it directly please escalate it to the right team right away.

Understood — I’m treating this as urgent. I checked the audit trail and the user was deactivated in our app, but your Google IdP continued asserting membership in the 'Support-App-Users' group until 08:43 UTC, which allowed a fresh session. I’ve revoked all active sessions for that user on our side. Your offboarding workflow is triggered only when the user status changes from Active to Suspended inside the app, and because the user was provisioned through SCIM the state moved directly to Deactivated, so that automation never fired. I’ve escalated this to our identity engineering team to review the SSO/SCIM behavior and to our workflow team for a safer offboarding trigger recommendation.
Thanks. Is there anything we should change immediately so this doesn't happen again for the next termination?

Yes. Until the specialists finish their review, please remove users from the Google access group before deprovisioning them, and update your offboarding automation to trigger on both Suspended and Deactivated statuses if your plan supports multi-condition triggers. I’ve attached those recommendations to the escalation and marked it as a security-impacting case.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Can’t remove former contractor from workspace
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: I’m trying to remove a contractor who finished last month, but every time I click deactivate I get a spinning loader and then nothing changes. I also noticed her name still appears as the owner of two automation rules, so I’m wondering if that is blocking the account change.

comments.message: The contractor is still listed as Editor. I tried from Chrome and Firefox, and both show the same endless spinner when deactivating the account.

If the user owns automation rules, that can sometimes prevent deletion. Please reassign those rules to another admin first, then try again. If the spinner continues, open DevTools and let me know whether there are any network errors.
I reassigned both rules, but the deactivate button still spins forever. In DevTools I see a failed POST returning 403.
A 403 means your browser is blocking the request, likely due to an extension or stale cookies. Please disable extensions and clear cookies for our site, then try again.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Can’t remove former contractor from report recipients
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: I’m cleaning up access after a contractor left. I deactivated the user successfully, but their email is still listed as a recipient on three automated reports, and the system won’t let me remove it because it says the schedule is locked by the report owner. The owner’s role was also changed last month and she no longer has admin rights. How do I update report recipients without reactivating everyone?

comments.message: I’m cleaning up access after a contractor left. I deactivated the user successfully, but their email is still listed as a recipient on three automated reports, and the system won’t let me remove it because it says the schedule is locked by the report owner. The owner’s role was also changed last month and she no longer has admin rights. How do I update report recipients without reactivating everyone?

Scheduled report recipients can usually be edited only by the current schedule owner or an admin with reporting management rights. Since the original owner lost admin rights, the cleanest path is to have a current admin transfer schedule ownership to themselves, then remove the contractor from the recipient list. You should not need to reactivate the contractor account.
I’m a current admin, but I don’t see a transfer option on the schedule. Only pause, duplicate, and delete.

Understood. In some plans the ownership transfer control isn’t exposed in the schedule drawer and has to be done from the report detail page or by our support team. I can’t complete that from chat, so I’m escalating this to our reporting specialists with the report IDs if you send them over. In the meantime, duplicating the schedule under your account and deleting the old one is a workaround if the recipients need to be updated today.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Parent-child workflow updates are causing an automation loop
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: We automated a process where updating a parent project should update all child tasks, and then child task completion should update the parent project status. Since turning both workflows on, records keep bouncing between statuses and hitting our automation limits. I need advice on how to stop the loop without losing the sync between parent and child records.

comments.message: Workflow A: when parent project status changes to In Progress, set all child tasks to In Progress. Workflow B: when all child tasks are In Progress, update parent project to In Progress. We assumed no-op updates would be ignored, but the logs show both workflows repeatedly firing.

You've created a circular trigger pattern. Even if the visible status value doesn't appear to change, downstream updates can still count as modifications and retrigger automations. The safest approach is to add guard conditions, such as a source marker field like 'Updated by automation,' or require the new status to differ from the current stored value before writing. Many teams also separate parent-to-child sync from child-to-parent rollups so only one direction performs direct status writes.
We don't have a source marker yet, but we can add one. Is that enough by itself?

Usually it helps, but you'll want to clear the marker carefully so you don't suppress legitimate future updates. I'd recommend implementing both a marker and explicit 'only update if value changed' checks, then testing in a sandbox before re-enabling both workflows in production.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Imported date format changed and automation skipped records
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: After yesterday's CSV import, our renewal date field shows correctly on the records, but the automation that should trigger 30 days before renewal skipped about 400 customers. Looking closer, the skipped records were imported with DD/MM/YYYY while our usual file is YYYY-MM-DD. The app normalized the display, so I assumed the data was fine. Could there be a parsing bug where automations stored the raw date differently from the record view?

comments.message: After yesterday's CSV import, our renewal date field shows correctly on the records, but the automation that should trigger 30 days before renewal skipped about 400 customers. Looking closer, the skipped records were imported with DD/MM/YYYY while our usual file is YYYY-MM-DD.

I reproduced this with one of the skipped customer IDs. The import layer normalized the renewal date for display, but the automation engine stored those rows with a null next-trigger value when the source format was DD/MM/YYYY and locale detection was disabled. So yes, this is a bug affecting the downstream automation data, not just formatting. I have reprocessed the affected import batch so the trigger values are being recalculated now.
Will that also backfill the automations that should have fired yesterday?

It will recalculate eligibility, but missed actions from yesterday will not automatically replay. I've submitted a one-time backfill request to run the renewal workflow for the affected 400 records, and our automation team will confirm once that's complete.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: New teammate can't access our workspace after invite
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: I invited a new project coordinator to our workspace yesterday, but she still can't get in. She says the link sends her to a sign-in page and then she gets a message that no account is associated with our organization. I'm also not fully sure whether I added her with the right role, because I only wanted her to view reports and comment, not edit settings. Can you help me get her access sorted out and explain the correct way to add people with limited permissions?

comments.message: I invited a new project coordinator to our workspace yesterday, but she still can't get in. She says the link sends her to a sign-in page and then she gets a message that no account is associated with our organization. I'm also not fully sure whether I added her with the right role, because I only wanted her to view reports and comment, not edit settings. Can you help me get her access sorted out and explain the correct way to add people with limited permissions?

Absolutely — it sounds like there may be two things going on: the invite may not have been accepted under the same email address you invited, and the assigned role may need adjusting. Please go to Admin Settings > Users, find her pending invite, and confirm the email address matches the one she used to sign in. If it doesn't, delete the pending invite and resend it to the correct address. For limited permissions, assign the Viewer role if she only needs to see reports, or Commenter if she should also leave comments. Neither role can edit settings.

I checked and the invite was sent to her personal Gmail, but she tried signing in with her work Microsoft account. I resent it to the work address. She can now get in, but she can still see the user management menu. I thought viewers wouldn't see that.

Glad the login issue is fixed. If she can see User Management, she was likely added as a Manager instead of Viewer or Commenter. In Admin Settings > Users, click her profile, change the role to Commenter, and save. Commenters can access shared content and leave comments but cannot manage users or workspace settings. If she still sees the menu after the role change, ask her to sign out and back in so the permissions refresh.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Restored user records lost team assignments
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: We restored five deactivated user profiles from the recycle bin after an HR sync mistake. Their names and email addresses came back, but their team assignments and territory mappings are blank. Before I manually rebuild everything, I need to know if restore is supposed to bring related assignment data back or if that linkage was dropped incorrectly.

comments.message: We restored five deactivated user profiles from the recycle bin after an HR sync mistake. Their names and email addresses came back, but their team assignments and territory mappings are blank. Before I manually rebuild everything, I need to know if restore is supposed to bring related assignment data back or if that linkage was dropped incorrectly.
Restoring users should generally bring back related configuration unless it was deleted separately. Can you provide one affected user ID and roughly when the restore was done?
One example is U-8821. All five were restored around 9:15 AM Eastern today from the recycle bin by our admin.

I checked user U-8821 and can see the profile record was restored, but I’m not seeing any team assignment rows recreated. I suspect the restore job only rebuilt the core user table and skipped the related mapping tables. I don’t have tooling to repopulate those links from support, so I’m escalating this to our data integrity team. In the meantime, you can manually reassign teams, but if the original mappings are needed we’ll have to see whether engineering can recover them from backups.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Can’t demote former admin because member page crashes
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: We need to remove admin rights from an employee who moved departments, but whenever I open his member details panel the page spins for a few seconds and then throws a generic error banner. I can still open other users fine. I’ve tried from two admin accounts, so right now we’re stuck with the wrong permissions on his account.

comments.message: We need to remove admin rights from an employee who moved departments, but whenever I open his member details panel the page spins for a few seconds and then throws a generic error banner. I can still open other users fine. I’ve tried from two admin accounts, so right now we’re stuck with the wrong permissions on his account.

Thanks for reporting this. To troubleshoot, can you confirm whether that user was originally provisioned through SCIM or invited manually, and whether the error appears after filtering by team or only from the full member list?
He was provisioned through SCIM, and the error happens from both the full list and search. We also noticed he has no department listed anymore, which seems odd.

I checked similar cases and this looks tied to a malformed team reference on some SCIM-managed profiles. That would explain why opening the record crashes before you can change the role. I’ve filed it as a bug and asked engineering to repair the broken membership object on this specific account. Until they do, we can’t safely demote him from the UI. I’ve also suggested temporarily suspending his login via your identity provider if access needs to be restricted immediately.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: API returns 404 for users that clearly exist in the UI
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: I'm trying to automate role audits through your API. I can sign in to the app and see the users in the admin panel, but when I call GET /v1/users/{email} for several employees the API returns 404. It looks like an access problem, but the docs say admins can retrieve users. We recently moved everyone to SAML login, so I'm wondering whether account identifiers changed.

comments.message: For example, I can see maria.chen@company.com in the UI, but GET /v1/users/maria.chen@company.com returns not found. My token belongs to an admin in the same workspace.
The API may not support email addresses in that path parameter. Please try URL-encoding the @ sign and periods and see if that fixes it.
I already tried encoding. Still 404. Searching by name in the UI works, and the user definitely exists.

Understood. In your tenant, SAML is set to use immutable external IDs as the primary user key, and the /v1/users/{id} endpoint expects the platform user ID, not email. Your admin login access is fine; this is just the wrong identifier for the API route. Use GET /v1/users?email=maria.chen@company.com first, then call the detail endpoint with the returned user ID.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: New hires can’t accept invitations after domain verification
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Since we verified our company domain yesterday, all new hire invitations are landing on the signup page with an error saying the account can’t be joined. Existing users are fine. I’m an org admin and this is blocking onboarding for six people starting this morning.

comments.message: Since we verified our company domain yesterday, all new hire invitations are landing on the signup page with an error saying the account can’t be joined. Existing users are fine. I’m an org admin and this is blocking onboarding for six people starting this morning.

Sorry about that. When this happens after domain verification, it is often because auto-join is enabled alongside invitation-only restrictions. Please go to Admin > Security and check whether both 'Allow users with verified domain to join automatically' and 'Require admin invitation' are turned on. That combination can cause invite redemption conflicts for new users.
Yes, both are on. If I turn off auto-join, will that affect current users signing in through SSO?

Current users will still be able to sign in normally through SSO. Turning off auto-join only stops new accounts from trying to self-provision during invite redemption. Please disable auto-join, resend one invitation to test, and if it works you can resend the rest. If you prefer to keep auto-join on, I can escalate this because the invite flow should handle that combination better.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Annual renewal processed early and broke our approval workflows

I need someone to verify the billing renewal date, confirm whether our workflow automation entitlements changed, and restore the features we had before this happened.

comments.message: We were charged for annual renewal 26 days early, and now several approval workflows say our plan does not support advanced conditions. Nothing was changed on our side.

I’m sorry about that. I can see a renewal invoice was generated after an amendment synced from your sales order, but I’m not able to verify the contract dates from support. On the automation side, the logs show entitlement checks failing for steps that require the Advanced Automation package.
We definitely had that package last week, and no one approved any downgrade.

Understood. I’ve escalated this to both Billing Operations and Account Support because it looks like the early renewal may have applied the wrong product bundle. I can’t restore entitlements manually, but I’ve marked it as urgent since your production workflows are blocked.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Manager-based reminders not sending after permission cleanup
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: We cleaned up old admin permissions and disabled several inactive accounts, and now our reminder workflow for overdue approvals is not sending messages to managers for one business unit. The requests are still being created, but the escalation reminders never go out. I need to understand whether this is tied to user records, notification settings, or the workflow itself.

comments.message: Only the Manufacturing unit is affected. The reminder step is configured to notify the requester's manager after 48 hours. For impacted users, the requests stay pending and no reminder appears in the audit log.
Please check whether email notifications are enabled globally under Settings. If they're off, reminders won't send.
Email notifications are enabled globally, and other business units are still getting reminders. It seems specific to manager routing for Manufacturing users.
Then it may be a temporary delay in the notification service. Please wait 24 hours and see if the reminders appear.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Need refund after duplicate user accounts inflated our seat count
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Our HR team imported users twice, which created duplicate accounts for several employees with slightly different email aliases. I merged or removed the duplicates in user administration, but we were already billed for 11 extra seats this month. Can those charges be reversed?

comments.message: Examples are john.doe@company.com and jdoe@company.com. The duplicates were active for about five days before we caught them. I’ve deleted the extra accounts now, but invoice preview still includes 11 additional seats.

I reviewed the audit trail and can confirm the duplicate users were created through two separate CSV imports. Because your plan bills on the highest active seat count reached during the cycle, the seat spike is reflected correctly on the invoice.
So even obvious duplicates can’t be adjusted?

Support can’t automatically waive usage-based charges, but I do think this is a reasonable refund request. I’ve sent the billing team the user IDs removed in the merge and asked them to review an exception for the 11 excess seats. No guarantee, but they’ll reply by email.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Deleted user still appears in API exports and group membership counts
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: I removed several former employees from the admin panel, but they still appear in our API export and inflate group membership totals used by our audit script. Some records look soft-deleted, others are inactive, and one account still has an API key attached. I need to understand the lifecycle states and make sure only the right admins can perform permanent removals because this is getting messy.

comments.message: Our audit compares active user counts by group. After deleting five users in the UI, GET /groups/{id}/members still returns them for two groups, and the user export includes deleted_at for some but not all.
Once a user is deleted, they should disappear completely from groups and exports within a few minutes. If they still show up, please clear your cache and rerun the call.
This is server-side API output, not a browser cache issue. Also, one of the users cannot be hard-deleted because the UI says they still own an API key and imported files.

Understood. I oversimplified earlier. In our system, inactive, soft-deleted, and permanently deleted users behave differently in exports and membership endpoints, especially when owned artifacts still exist. A user with active API keys or imported assets may be blocked from permanent deletion until those objects are revoked or transferred. Soft-deleted users can still appear in some audit endpoints unless you filter for active=true. Permanent removal is typically restricted to higher-level admins for that reason. If you want, I can escalate this with your account IDs so we can confirm which records are stuck behind owned artifacts.

subject: Offboarded users still appear in Jira integration assignee list

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: No se actualizan los estados en Jira después de cambiar el flujo
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Tenemos sincronización bidireccional con Jira. La semana pasada cambiamos el flujo de estados en nuestro proyecto y desde entonces, cuando una incidencia se mueve a "En validación" en su plataforma, en Jira se queda en el estado anterior. Otras actualizaciones sí pasan, como comentarios y prioridad. ¿Qué puede estar bloqueando solo los estados?

comments.message: Revisé la conexión general y está sana. Parece algo específico del mapeo del workflow, pero no estoy seguro.

Correcto, cuando solo fallan los estados después de un cambio de workflow, lo primero es revisar el mapeo de estados porque los nombres visibles pueden seguir iguales aunque el ID interno cambie. Entre en Integraciones > Jira > Mapeo de estados y verifique si "En validación" aparece como no asignado o vinculado a un estado archivado.
Sí, aparece como no asignado. El estado viejo fue reemplazado y nunca lo remapeamos.

Ahí está la causa. Asigne "En validación" al nuevo estado de Jira y guarde los cambios. Después haga una transición manual en un ticket de prueba para confirmar que la sincronización vuelve a funcionar. Tenga en cuenta que los tickets que fallaron antes no siempre se reenvían solos, así que quizá deba volver a cambiarles el estado para disparar el evento otra vez.

comments.message: During a user cleanup, our IT team accidentally deactivated my owner account instead of an old contractor. Verification passed. I’ve granted Billing Admin to your operations manager and sent them an invite to set a password. best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: No entiendo cómo limitar qué datos pasan a un ERP por la integración
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Estamos explorando una integración con nuestro ERP mediante API, pero me preocupa que se envíen más datos de los necesarios. Quiero una explicación básica de cómo se filtran registros, qué se puede mapear y cómo hacer pruebas sin afectar producción. No necesito que me lo implementen todavía; necesito entender el enfoque correcto.

comments.message: El equipo financiero solo quiere que viajen clientes aprobados y ciertos campos de facturación. Yo veo opciones de filtros, eventos y payloads, pero no sé cómo combinarlas.

Lo ideal es pensar la integración en tres capas. Primero, el disparador: por ejemplo, solo cuando un cliente cambia a estado 'aprobado'. Segundo, el filtro: excluir registros sin NIF o sin dirección fiscal completa. Tercero, el mapeo: enviar únicamente los campos necesarios al ERP, como razón social, identificador fiscal y condiciones de pago.
¿Y cómo probamos eso sin mandar datos reales al ERP?

Hazlo en un entorno de prueba o con un endpoint sandbox del ERP. Si no lo tienen, pueden usar temporalmente un receptor de pruebas para inspeccionar el payload antes de activar producción. También recomiendo probar con 3-5 registros representativos: uno válido, uno incompleto y uno que debería quedar excluido. Así aprenden si la lógica de filtros está funcionando antes de abrir el flujo completo.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Cargo prorrateado inesperado y error 500 al crear usuarios
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Añadí 3 usuarios nuevos y nos llegó un cargo prorrateado que no esperábamos porque pensábamos que se facturaba al siguiente ciclo. Desde ese mismo momento, cuando intento crear más usuarios desde el panel de administración, aparece un error 500. Probé en dos navegadores y con otro administrador.

comments.message: Añadí 3 usuarios nuevos y nos llegó un cargo prorrateado que no esperábamos porque pensábamos que se facturaba al siguiente ciclo. Desde ese mismo momento, cuando intento crear más usuarios desde el panel de administración, aparece un error 500. Probé en dos navegadores y con otro administrador.

Hola. En los planes mensuales, los asientos extra se cobran prorrateados de inmediato, así que ese cargo es esperado. Respecto al error 500, ¿podrían compartir la hora exacta y un correo de uno de los usuarios que intentaron crear? Quiero revisar los logs del backend.
Fue hoy sobre las 10:14 CET. Intenté crear a lucia@grupoalbor.es y luego a soporte2@grupoalbor.es. Necesito saber si el cargo se puede revertir porque aún no empezarán hasta el mes que viene.

Desactivé temporalmente esa validación y ya deberían poder crearlos. Sobre el cargo prorrateado, en este tipo de plan no se revierte automáticamente, pero si quitan esos asientos antes de la próxima renovación puedo solicitar una nota de crédito excepcional. No está garantizada.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Se mezclaron registros entre dos datasets después de cambiar la clave primaria
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Ayer cambiamos la clave primaria de un dataset de "email" a "customer_id" porque había muchos correos compartidos. Después de eso, varias filas quedaron asociadas al cliente equivocado y algunos registros antiguos desaparecieron de las vistas filtradas. Necesito ayuda para entender si el sistema reindexó mal o si perdimos relaciones al cambiar la clave.

comments.message: Ayer cambiamos la clave primaria de un dataset de "email" a "customer_id" porque había muchos correos compartidos. Después de eso, varias filas quedaron asociadas al cliente equivocado y algunos registros antiguos desaparecieron de las vistas filtradas. Necesito ayuda para entender si el sistema reindexó mal o si perdimos relaciones al cambiar la clave.

Cuando se cambia la clave primaria, el sistema vuelve a reconciliar registros y las relaciones dependientes pueden reescribirse usando el nuevo índice. Si había filas con customer_id vacío, duplicado o con formato inconsistente, es posible que se hayan colapsado registros o roto asociaciones. Lo primero es validar un muestreo de IDs afectados y revisar en el log de reindexación cuántos conflictos detectó el proceso.
Eso no lo puedo ver yo desde la interfaz. Solo veo que el cambio terminó "correctamente".

Tiene razón, ese log no es visible para todos los roles. Voy a pedir al equipo técnico una revisión del reindexado y si encuentran conflictos podrán indicarnos si conviene restaurar el índice anterior o relanzar la reconciliación con una tabla limpia de customer_id. Envíeme 5-10 ejemplos de registros mezclados y la hora aproximada del cambio para acotar la auditoría.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Custom admin role can edit users but not build headcount report
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: I set up a custom admin role for our HR operations lead. The role works for adding and deactivating users in her business unit, but she gets an error when trying to create a headcount report from the employee dataset. She can view an existing headcount dashboard just fine. I’m not sure if custom user administration roles inherit reporting limitations by default or if there’s a missing permission toggle somewhere.

comments.message: I set up a custom admin role for our HR operations lead. The role works for adding and deactivating users in her business unit, but she gets an error when trying to create a headcount report from the employee dataset. She can view an existing headcount dashboard just fine. I’m not sure if custom user administration roles inherit reporting limitations by default or if there’s a missing permission toggle somewhere.

Viewing dashboards and building reports are controlled separately. A custom admin role can absolutely allow user administration while still blocking report creation if the reporting-builder permission or dataset access wasn’t included. Please review the custom role and any attached permission groups for: report creation, dataset access to Employee/Headcount, and export rights if needed.
I see report viewing and export, but not report creation. If I turn on report creation, will she be able to build reports for all business units?

Not necessarily. Report creation lets her build reports, but the data scope still follows the datasets and row-level access assigned to her account. If her business-unit filter is configured correctly, she should only be able to build headcount reports for that unit while continuing to manage users there. After enabling the permission, test with a simple employee count report to confirm the scope is correct.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Need report access retained while changing regional admin structure
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: We’re restructuring our account so two regional admins will become one global admin plus three site managers. I need to change the user roles this week, but each of those people owns custom reports used by their sites. I’m worried the role changes will either break the reports or expose data they shouldn’t see afterward. Can you outline the safest order of operations so we preserve reporting and keep user administration aligned with the new org structure?

comments.message: We’re restructuring our account so two regional admins will become one global admin plus three site managers. I need to change the user roles this week, but each of those people owns custom reports used by their sites. I’m worried the role changes will either break the reports or expose data they shouldn’t see afterward. Can you outline the safest order of operations so we preserve reporting and keep user administration aligned with the new org structure?

Yes — the safest sequence is: 1) inventory which reports and schedules each current admin owns, 2) transfer ownership of any cross-site reports to the future global admin before role changes, 3) confirm each site manager has a role that includes only site-level user administration plus access to the specific reports they need, and 4) only then downgrade the old regional admins. If a report uses data outside a site manager’s eventual scope, it may still exist after transfer but will render partially or fail for that manager unless it’s redesigned.
That helps. Is there a way to test what a site manager will see before we finalize the role changes?

If you have the impersonation feature, use that after assigning the draft role to a pilot user account; otherwise, create a temporary test user with the same site-manager role and reporting groups. Review both user admin pages and the key reports before making the production changes. If you send a list of critical reports, I can suggest which ones are most likely to break due to scope changes.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Bulk role change triggered the wrong approval path
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: I did a bulk update to move several users from Contractor to Employee, and now expense requests from some of them are still following the contractor approval workflow while others use the employee workflow. We also renamed a permission role during the same cleanup, so I'm unsure whether the automation is reading the user type, the access role, or stale data.

comments.message: For context, our workflow should route Contractors to procurement review and Employees to manager approval. After the bulk change, six users switched correctly, but four still hit procurement even though their profiles say Employee and they have employee access.

This can happen when the workflow condition is tied to a different user attribute than the one updated in bulk. In some environments, 'employment type' and 'security role' are separate fields. If you renamed the permission role but only changed employment type for some users, the automation may still be evaluating the old contractor-related field. Can you check one affected user's audit history to see which attributes changed, and confirm what exact field the workflow condition references?

I checked. The affected users had Employment Type updated, but the workflow condition is actually based on a custom profile field called Worker Category. That field stayed as Contractor for those four users.

There we go. Update Worker Category for the affected users or change the workflow to evaluate Employment Type if that's now your source of truth. Also review whether the renamed permission role is used anywhere else, but for this routing issue the mismatch between user administration fields and workflow configuration is the root cause.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: CSV import fails on row 214 with invalid date error
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: I'm importing a customer list via CSV and the upload keeps failing on row 214 with an invalid date error. The date in that row looks normal to me, and I already re-saved the file as UTF-8 CSV. The import screen doesn't tell me which field is the problem, so I'm stuck troubleshooting this blind.

comments.message: I'm importing a customer list via CSV and the upload keeps failing on row 214 with an invalid date error. The date in that row looks normal to me, and I already re-saved the file as UTF-8 CSV. The import screen doesn't tell me which field is the problem, so I'm stuck troubleshooting this blind.
Usually that means the system wants MM/DD/YYYY. Please change the date format in that row and re-upload the file.
The value is already 03/14/2024, so it's already in that format. I also removed the whole row and then it failed on the next row with the same message.
In that case the file may just be corrupted. Please create a new CSV from scratch and try again.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: How do I hand off admin duties while on parental leave?
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: I'm going on parental leave for four months and need someone else to handle user invites, permission changes, and account notices while I'm away. I want to understand the difference between making my colleague an admin versus changing the primary owner/contact. We don't need a permanent transfer if it's avoidable.

comments.message: Is there a temporary delegation option, or do I have to fully transfer ownership? We mainly need coverage for account changes and onboarding new hires while I'm out.

There isn't a temporary vacation delegate feature for ownership in most accounts, but you may not need a full transfer. In many cases, adding a trusted colleague as admin is enough for user invites, permission updates, and day-to-day workspace management. Ownership or primary account contact changes are usually only needed for sensitive account-level actions like subscription changes, renewal communications, or certain billing settings.
That helps. If I leave myself as owner but change the primary contact, will they get admin alerts too?

They'll receive the account-level communications tied to that contact field, but operational/admin alerts depend on the specific notification type and the user's in-product role. A practical setup is: keep you as owner, grant your colleague admin rights, update the primary account contact if they should receive renewal/account notices, and test one admin workflow before you leave. If your plan includes settings restricted only to the owner, reply with your plan tier and I can flag whether a temporary ownership change should be considered.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Webhook says user removed, but API still shows them as active
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: We're listening for your user.updated webhook to revoke downstream access when a user is removed from our account. The webhook payload said the user was deactivated, but when our workflow called the user API immediately after, the response still showed active=true for about a minute. Is that expected, and how should we handle this race condition in our user administration process?

comments.message: This caused a downstream system to re-enable the account because it trusted the follow-up API read over the webhook event. We're trying to make offboarding deterministic.

Thanks for flagging this. There can be a short eventual-consistency delay between the event bus and the read replica that serves some API GET requests, especially right after deactivation. The webhook is emitted from the primary write path, so it may arrive before all reads reflect the new status. We recommend treating the webhook as authoritative for revocation and retrying the GET with exponential backoff for up to 2 minutes if you need to confirm state.
Understood. We'll change our workflow so the webhook triggers revocation immediately and the API check becomes a delayed verification step.
That’s the safest pattern here. If you'd like, I can also share the event ordering notes for user.updated versus group membership events so your offboarding flow stays consistent across both.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: QuickBooks sync broken and locked-out admin after card update failed
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: I tried to update our payment card yesterday after the old one expired. The payment page errored out twice, and today our QuickBooks integration is disconnected and I can’t log in with my normal admin account anymore because it says my role was suspended. Another invoice email came through this morning showing both the failed card and a successful charge on the new card, so I need help understanding whether we’re delinquent, double charged, or both. I need admin access restored because nobody else on the team can reconnect QuickBooks.

comments.message: I tried to update our payment card yesterday after the old one expired. The payment page errored out twice, and today our QuickBooks integration is disconnected and I can’t log in with my normal admin account anymore because it says my role was suspended. Another invoice email came through this morning showing both the failed card and a successful charge on the new card, so I need help understanding whether we’re delinquent, double charged, or both. I need admin access restored because nobody else on the team can reconnect QuickBooks.

I can see one failed payment attempt on the expired card and one successful capture on the replacement card, so you were not double charged. However, the dunning workflow briefly marked the workspace past due and suspended one admin role, which in your case was also the QuickBooks integration owner. I’ve restored your admin access and cleared the delinquency flag. Please sign back in, reconnect QuickBooks from the Integrations page, and the failed sync jobs should replay automatically within the hour.
I’m back in now, but reconnecting QuickBooks says the app is still owned by a disabled user.

Thanks for checking. The account-level suspension is lifted, but the integration ownership record didn’t update with it. I’ve reassigned the QuickBooks connection to your restored admin user and queued a backfill for the last 36 hours of missed invoices. If any entries are still missing after that completes, reply here and I’ll pull the sync logs manually.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Former employee still receiving password reset emails
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: I’m our workspace admin and I deactivated a sales rep last week, but she’s still getting password reset emails from your system and her name still appears in the people picker on some projects. I need to make sure her access is fully removed without breaking the records she owned. What should I do?

comments.message: I’m our workspace admin and I deactivated a sales rep last week, but she’s still getting password reset emails from your system and her name still appears in the people picker on some projects. I need to make sure her access is fully removed without breaking the records she owned.

Thanks for the details. If the user is deactivated but still appears in search or project history, that is usually expected for audit purposes, but they should not be able to sign in. Please go to Admin Settings > Users, open her profile, and confirm the status is Deactivated rather than Pending Invitation. Also check whether SSO is enabled, because identity-provider initiated password emails can come from your IdP rather than from us.

We do use SSO, but the email she forwarded has your branding and a reset link on your domain. In her profile I also see an old secondary email address, and the status says Deactivated. We want to keep ownership history but stop any notifications entirely.

Understood. In that case, please revoke all active sessions from the user profile, remove the secondary email address, and unassign her from any automation rules or approval steps that still reference her account. Deactivated users can remain visible in historical pickers, but they should stop receiving system emails once sessions are revoked and notification targets are cleared. If you still see new reset emails after that, reply with the timestamp and I’ll escalate it to our auth team to inspect the mail logs.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Merged duplicate users and API calls now fail for one account ID
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: I merged two user accounts in our tenant because a colleague had duplicate logins, and after that our integration started failing when it tries to update records linked to the old user ID. Some API calls return 404 and others return a validation error saying the account reference is inactive. I need to understand how account merges affect API identifiers and whether there is a redirect or mapping we can query.

comments.message: After merging a duplicate user into the correct account, our integration can no longer PATCH resources that still reference the old user ID. Is there an endpoint to translate old IDs to the surviving account automatically?
Merges generally keep the old ID active as an alias for a period of time, so your integration should continue working. Please clear any local cache and retry the requests after 15 minutes.
We waited over an hour and the old ID still returns 404 on GET /users/{id}. The update payload also fails when that ID is included as owner_id on tickets.

Thanks for checking. I may have oversimplified that behavior. In most cases, the retired user ID is not queryable after a merge, and there isn't a public lookup endpoint for old-to-new IDs. The practical fix is to fetch the surviving user record from the UI or export, update your local mapping table, and stop sending the deprecated ID. I'm escalating this internally to confirm whether your plan includes the merge audit API, which would make that mapping easier to retrieve.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: La importación desde Google Sheets falla con columnas personalizadas
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Estoy usando la integración con Google Sheets para cargar oportunidades desde una hoja compartida. La conexión se hizo bien, pero cuando programo la importación automática, las columnas estándar entran sin problema y las personalizadas quedan vacías o se corren de lugar. Puede que haya configurado mal los nombres internos de los campos en el producto, porque cambiamos varias etiquetas hace poco.

comments.message: Las columnas "Etapa", "Origen" y "Ejecutivo" se importan bien, pero "Segmento comercial" y "Prioridad interna" llegan en blanco aunque en la hoja sí tienen datos.

Esto suele pasar cuando el nombre visible del campo cambió pero la importación sigue buscando la clave interna anterior. Vaya a Configuración > Campos de oportunidad, abra esos campos y copie el nombre API o identificador interno. Luego, en la integración de Google Sheets, remapee las columnas contra esos campos exactos.
Hice el remapeo y una de las columnas ya carga, pero "Prioridad interna" sigue entrando en blanco.

Si ese campo es de tipo selección o depende de valores permitidos, la integración solo importará opciones que coincidan exactamente con la configuración del producto. Revise en Configuración > Campos de oportunidad si los valores en la lista son idénticos a los de la hoja, incluyendo mayúsculas y espacios. Si quiere, también puede convertir temporalmente el campo a texto para validar que el problema es de opciones y no de conexión.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: No me queda claro cómo funciona el mapeo de campos con HubSpot
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Estoy conectando HubSpot y veo opciones de mapeo estándar, campos personalizados y reglas de sobrescritura, pero no entiendo cuándo un dato se actualiza y cuándo no. Me preocupa romper información existente. ¿Pueden explicármelo de forma simple?

comments.message: Tenemos un campo personalizado de 'segmento' en HubSpot y otro parecido aquí. Si activo la sincronización, no sé cuál gana cuando los valores son distintos.
No te preocupes, eso no debería causar problemas. En general, la plataforma simplemente mezcla los datos y toma el valor más reciente automáticamente.
No veo ninguna opción que diga eso. Solo veo 'preferir valor de HubSpot', 'preferir valor local' y 'no sobrescribir si existe'.

Entonces deja la opción por defecto y debería funcionar bien. Si hay conflictos, normalmente el sistema los resuelve solo. De todos modos, puedes probar en producción con algunos contactos para ver qué pasa.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: La integración con Mailchimp no deja mapear el consentimiento de marketing
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Estoy configurando Mailchimp para sincronizar contactos desde su plataforma y necesitamos enviar también el estado de consentimiento de marketing por razones legales. La integración conecta bien y sincroniza nombre y correo, pero el campo de consentimiento no aparece como opción para mapear. Revisé la audiencia en Mailchimp y también nuestros campos, pero no sé si ese atributo requiere alguna configuración especial en el producto.

comments.message: Necesitamos que cuando un contacto marque aceptación en nuestro formulario, ese consentimiento viaje a Mailchimp. Ahora mismo solo suben email y nombre.
Para que un campo aparezca en el mapeo, primero debe existir como campo personalizado en ambos sistemas. Cree un campo booleano llamado consentimiento y luego sincronice de nuevo el esquema.
Ya existe en ambos lados. En Mailchimp es parte del marketing permissions, no un campo personalizado normal.

Gracias, eso cambia el caso. Las marketing permissions en Mailchimp no se exponen como campos estándar en nuestro mapeador, así que no se pueden configurar desde la interfaz general. Voy a escalarlo al equipo de integraciones para confirmar si hay soporte mediante una opción avanzada o si requiere API personalizada. Mientras tanto, solo podría mantenerse manualmente o con un flujo externo.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: La integración con Jira no crea incidencias como esperaba
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Configuré la integración con Jira pensando que cada ticket con la etiqueta 'bug' iba a crear una incidencia automáticamente, pero solo pasa a veces. No entiendo si el problema está en el disparador, en los permisos del proyecto o en el mapeo. ¿Me ayudan a entender cómo debería funcionar?

comments.message: Hice tres pruebas: una creó incidencia, otra no hizo nada y la tercera falló con un mensaje genérico. Necesito saber qué revisar primero.

Primero revisa el flujo lógico: 1) que la regla se dispare cuando se añade la etiqueta 'bug', 2) que la conexión con Jira siga autorizada, y 3) que el proyecto y tipo de incidencia de destino acepten los campos obligatorios. Si falta, por ejemplo, 'Issue Type' o 'Project Key', la creación puede fallar aunque la regla se active.
Creo que no habíamos mapeado prioridad ni componente, pero no sabía que fueran obligatorios en ese proyecto.

Eso encaja con el comportamiento intermitente. En Jira, algunos proyectos tienen validaciones propias. Como siguiente paso educativo, te sugiero abrir la configuración del proyecto en Jira y anotar todos los campos requeridos para crear una incidencia de tipo Bug. Luego vuelve al mapeo aquí y completa esos campos. Si después de eso sigue fallando, envíanos el ID de una ejecución y revisamos el log contigo.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Password resets disabled after bulk user import
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: We imported 43 new users with the CSV tool yesterday. They all appear in the directory, but multiple people are saying they can’t set a password. The reset email either doesn’t arrive or the link says expired immediately. I need to figure out whether the import created the accounts incorrectly and what I should do from the admin side.

comments.message: These are not SSO users. We imported name, email, and role only. In User Administration most of them show 'Invited,' but a few somehow show 'Active' even though they have never signed in.

Bulk-imported users should receive a standard invite flow, not a pre-set password flow. If some appear Active before first login, it may mean they were matched to existing dormant accounts. Please export the user list and check for duplicate emails or prior deactivated users that were reactivated during import.
I found several duplicates tied to old deactivated contractors. What should I do with the broken invites for the rest?

For the matched dormant accounts, reset access from each user profile so they receive a fresh activation email rather than a generic password reset. For users still in 'Invited' status, cancel the pending invite in bulk and resend from the import summary page; that generates new tokens. If mail delivery is still failing after that, send me 2-3 example addresses and I’ll have our email team check bounce logs.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Error al sincronizar contactos con HubSpot
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Estoy intentando conectar nuestra cuenta con HubSpot para sincronizar contactos, pero desde ayer la sincronización se queda en "En curso" y nunca termina. Ya reconecté la integración y probé con otro navegador. Necesito saber si el problema es de su plataforma o de los campos mapeados porque ventas no está recibiendo los leads nuevos.

comments.message: La sincronización empezó a fallar después de que agregamos un campo personalizado obligatorio en HubSpot. En su panel no veo ningún error claro, solo que la última ejecución quedó colgada hace 14 horas.

Gracias por los detalles. Cuando se agrega un campo obligatorio en HubSpot, nuestra integración puede detenerse si ese campo no está mapeado o no recibe un valor válido. Por favor vaya a Integraciones > HubSpot > Mapeo de campos y revise si el nuevo campo aparece con advertencias. También necesito que me confirme si el error ocurre en todos los contactos o solo en los nuevos.
Revisé el mapeo y el campo nuevo ni siquiera aparece en la lista. Solo falla con contactos nuevos; los antiguos sí se siguen viendo.

Eso apunta a que el esquema en caché de la integración no se actualizó después del cambio en HubSpot.
