Ya forcé una actualización del conector desde nuestro lado y le pedí al sistema que vuelva a cargar los campos. Espere unos 10 minutos, luego vuelva a abrir el mapeo y ejecute una sincronización manual. Si el campo aparece, asígnelo a un valor por defecto o a una propiedad existente para que los nuevos contactos no queden bloqueados.

subject: Former employee still appears active but can't sign in

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Can’t log in after changing primary email address
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: I changed my login email in my profile from my old company address to my new one after our rebrand. Since then I can no longer sign in consistently. Sometimes the old email says no account found, and the new email accepts the password but sends me back to the sign-in screen. I’m confused about which address is actually tied to my account now and whether your email change flow is broken.

comments.message: I can still see automated notifications coming to the new address, so part of the change seems to have worked.

It sounds like the email update may have only changed your notification address and not your login identity. Our system keeps those separate in some plans. Please continue signing in with your old email and use the new one only for notifications.
That doesn't match what the settings page said. It specifically said 'Primary email used for sign-in and notifications,' and now the old email isn't accepted either.

You’re right, and I gave you an incomplete answer. I tested this in a sandbox and found a bug affecting email changes on accounts created before the unified identity update: the visible profile email updates, but the authentication record may not finish migrating. I’ve opened a bug with engineering and attached your case. As a temporary workaround, I’ve sent a secure account recovery link to your new address so you can rebind the login identity. After you complete that flow, please sign in only with the new email.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Changed company name but contract emails still go to former controller
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Our company was acquired last month, and I'm trying to update the legal entity details and the main account contact. Right now invoice reminders and renewal notices are still going to our former controller, who no longer works here. I also need to understand whether changing the account owner will affect our team's admin access.

comments.message: I updated our company display name in the app, but contract-related emails are still going to the old finance contact. Can you switch the account owner to me and confirm what else we should update?
If you changed the company name, the rest should usually follow automatically. The old emails may stop on their own after the system syncs.
It's been two weeks, and they are definitely still going to the old person. Also, I don't know if account owner and billing contact are the same in your system.

You're right to question that, and I should have been clearer. Display name changes do not automatically update account owner or billing/renewal contacts. Those are often separate records. I can explain the difference: the account owner/admin role affects in-product administration, while billing and contract contacts determine who receives financial and renewal communications. Because changing legal entity and contract contacts can touch subscription records, I'm escalating this to our billing/account operations team to update the authorized contacts securely. In the meantime, please send the new contact name, title, and email from your company domain, and keep your existing admins in place since admin access won't automatically transfer with the billing contact update.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Login page branding change seems to have broken invite acceptance
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: We customized the login page with our own domain and branding, and after that I changed the self-serve registration toggle for a campaign. Since then, invited users are hitting a message that registration is closed, even though they were directly invited. Existing users can sign in normally. I need to get invites working again without opening general signup.

comments.message: After setting up our branded login page and turning off self-serve registration, invited users can no longer accept their invites. They are being told registration is closed. Current users can still log in.

That is odd. Invite acceptance should not depend on the public registration setting. Can you share whether your custom domain is pointing to the correct workspace and whether 'Treat invites as registrations' is enabled in your onboarding configuration?
I found that option and it is enabled. We turned it on months ago for a partner rollout and forgot about it.

That explains the behavior. With 'Treat invites as registrations' enabled, invited users are routed through the registration gate, so disabling self-serve registration blocks them too. Turn that option off in Onboarding Configuration, resend one invite, and the user should be able to create their account without reopening public signup.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Po korekcie literówki w adresie nie mogę wejść na konto
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: W moim loginie była literówka i poprosiłem support o poprawienie adresu e-mail. Od tej zmiany nie działa ani stare, ani nowe logowanie. System raz pokazuje, że konto nie istnieje, a raz że hasło jest błędne.

comments.message: Nowy poprawny adres to j.kaczmarek@merxon.pl. Stary miał brakującą literę w nazwie domeny.
Rozumiem. Sprawdziłem tylko, że konto istnieje pod nowym adresem, więc problem powinien być rozwiązany. Proszę użyć opcji „Nie pamiętam hasła”.
Już to robiłem kilka razy i nie dostaję żadnej wiadomości resetującej.
W takim razie możliwe, że wiadomości są blokowane przez Państwa serwer pocztowy. Proszę skontaktować się z własnym działem IT.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Restored user came back without previous permissions
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: I reactivated a manager who returned from leave, but their account only came back as a basic member. Before leave they were able to manage their team’s users and approve access changes. I need to know whether role history can be restored automatically or if I have to rebuild their permissions manually.

comments.message: I restored a returning manager, but their old admin permissions didn’t come back. They’re just a basic member now.

That is expected in some cases. When a user is reactivated, previous elevated permissions may not be restored automatically depending on how the account was deactivated and whether roles were assigned directly or through synced groups. Can you confirm whether their manager permissions were manually assigned or provisioned from your identity provider? I’m escalating this to our account configuration team because they can check the user history and tell us whether the prior role can be recovered.
I’m not sure. The user existed long before we enabled group sync, so it might have been a manual assignment originally.

Got it. I added that to the escalation. If the role was direct, the configuration team may be able to confirm it from the audit trail; if it was group-based, they’ll likely point you to the current mapping source. For now, you can manually grant the least-privileged admin role needed for their work, but I’d wait on restoring broader access until the team verifies the historical assignment.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Chcę usunąć siebie z konta, ale jestem jedynym adminem
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Odchodzę z firmy i mam zostać usunięty z systemu, ale kiedy próbuję dezaktywować swoje konto, pojawia się błąd, że nie można usunąć ostatniego administratora. Firma chce zachować dostęp do danych, więc potrzebuję wiedzieć, co zrobić poprawnie.

comments.message: Nie mogę usunąć własnego konta, bo system blokuje ostatniego administratora. Nie mam już nikogo z rolą admina, a mój następca zaczyna dopiero za tydzień. Jak zamknąć temat zgodnie z procedurą?
W takiej sytuacji trzeba najpierw nadać uprawnienia administratora innej aktywnej osobie. Dopóki na koncie nie ma drugiego admina, system nie pozwoli Panu zdezaktywować siebie.
Czy mogę nadać rolę admina osobie, która jeszcze nie aktywowała zaproszenia?

Nie, nowa osoba musi najpierw zaakceptować zaproszenie i zalogować się przynajmniej raz. Jeśli to niemożliwe przed Pana odejściem, możemy tymczasowo odroczyć dezaktywację konta albo — po autoryzacji firmy — ręcznie przenieść własność konta na wskazaną osobę, gdy tylko aktywuje dostęp.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Role change keeps reverting after save
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: I’m trying to downgrade one teammate from Workspace Admin to Analyst. The save button works, I get the green confirmation toast, and then after a page reload he’s still an admin. We’ve attempted this from two different admin accounts and once through the bulk editor, with the same result each time.

comments.message: I’m trying to downgrade one teammate from Workspace Admin to Analyst. The save button works, I get the green confirmation toast, and then after a page reload he’s still an admin. We’ve attempted this from two different admin accounts and once through the bulk editor, with the same result each time.
Thanks. This is sometimes caused by an external identity sync restoring the prior role. Are you using SCIM or any HRIS sync that maps roles into the platform?
Yes, SCIM is enabled, but this user is excluded from role sync. The audit log shows my role_change event succeeded and then nothing else changes it back.

Got it. Then the role shouldn’t revert, and the green toast is misleading. I suspect the frontend is acknowledging the request before the policy check finishes. Could you send the user ID and timestamp of your latest attempt so we can inspect the failed mutation? I’m escalating this because we’ll likely need engineering to confirm whether the role update is being rejected or rolled back server-side.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Need reseller contact removed and direct billing reinstated
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: We recently ended our relationship with an external reseller who used to manage our subscription, but invoices are still going to them and our direct credit card was also charged this month. I need the reseller removed from the account, our internal admin rights restored, and help figuring out why both billing paths were used.

comments.message: Our former reseller, Blue Arch Systems, should no longer be attached to our account as of this quarter. Yet April's invoice was sent to them, and our company Amex was also charged directly for $2,050. Please remove the reseller contact, make me the billing admin, and explain whether we've been double billed.

I can see the account had a reseller-managed billing setup that appears to have been partially converted to direct billing. That can cause confusion, but not always duplicate charges. I'll need to escalate the reseller detachment and admin reassignment to our finance systems team because those records sync from a different backend.
Do you at least know whether the Amex charge is valid? We need to close our month-end books tomorrow.

I can confirm the direct Amex charge posted from our system, but I can't yet verify whether the reseller invoice was also collectible or just informational. I've marked the escalation as urgent and asked the finance systems team to review both billing channels and transfer billing control to you. They should respond within 1 business day.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Seat increase approved by workflow, but account changes didn't apply before invoice
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: We set up an internal automation so any seat increase request gets manager approval and then updates our subscription through your API. A request for 12 additional seats was approved, but only 5 were actually added to the account. Despite that, the invoice billed us for all 12, and several new hires still can’t log in because the account change only partially applied. I need to know whether the workflow submitted duplicate billing events or whether the subscription update failed on your side.

comments.message: The approval workflow shows success and passed quantity=12 through the API step. In the admin portal we only received 5 extra seats, but billing charged for 12 on the same day.

I checked the API logs and found two calls: one request to add 5 seats from a manual admin action, and a later automated request to add 12. Our billing ledger recorded the 12-seat increase immediately, but the provisioning service rejected part of that update because your plan’s departmental cap allowed only 30 seats without a contract amendment.
So why were we billed for seats we never got?

That shouldn’t have happened as a final state. I’ve opened a billing correction for the 7 unprovisioned seats and escalated the provisioning mismatch to engineering. In the meantime, the automation should be changed to read the current seat cap before submitting quantity changes, otherwise approvals can create billable requests that exceed your contractual limit.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Two people shared one login and now we need separate accounts
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Our support desk had two coordinators using the same generic login for the past month, which I realize was a bad idea. We now need to split that into separate named users, make sure each person can sign in individually, and keep the old records if possible. I'm not sure whether I should rename the existing user, create two new ones, or how to avoid messing up permissions. Can you walk me through the correct setup and any best practices?

comments.message: Our support desk had two coordinators using the same generic login for the past month, which I realize was a bad idea. We now need to split that into separate named users, make sure each person can sign in individually, and keep the old records if possible. I'm not sure whether I should rename the existing user, create two new ones, or how to avoid messing up permissions. Can you walk me through the correct setup and any best practices?

Yes — don't rename the shared login into one of the individuals, because that makes the audit trail misleading. The recommended approach is to deactivate the shared account for future login, create two separate named users, and assign each the appropriate role and team membership. Historical records created by the generic account will remain attributed to that original user unless you manually reassign ownership on open items. After inviting the two new users, have each person activate their own login and enable MFA if available.
If I deactivate the shared account, can I still transfer the open tasks from it to one of the new coordinators?

Yes. Deactivation blocks sign-in but doesn't remove the account from assignment tools. From the task list, filter by owner = shared account, bulk reassign open items to the new users, and then leave closed historical items untouched so reporting remains accurate. For permissions, give both users the same Coordinator role the shared account had only if they truly need identical access; otherwise this is a good chance to reduce privileges per person.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Merged two teams and now need to rename account structure
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: We recently merged our support and success teams into one department. I want to rename our workspace, update the default sender name, and change the primary account contact without confusing our agents. I'm not sure which changes are cosmetic and which ones affect logins, notifications, or existing reports.

comments.message: Can you walk me through what happens if I change the workspace name and primary account contact? We want the branding updated, but we don't want to break dashboards or user access.

Absolutely. The workspace name change is mostly cosmetic: it updates the name shown in the app and, depending on your setup, some customer-facing areas like email headers or portal branding. It does not change user login credentials. Changing the primary account contact updates who receives account notices such as renewal reminders and certain administrative emails, but it won't remove existing admins or alter permissions by itself.
What about our reports and historical tickets? We also need to update the default sender from Support Team to Client Operations.

Historical tickets and reports remain intact after a workspace rename; they stay associated with the same account. Updating the default sender name affects outgoing notifications going forward, but it doesn't retroactively rename old messages already sent. Best practice is to make these changes during a low-volume period, notify agents about the new branding, and then verify one test notification and one dashboard after the update. If you want, I can also outline the exact admin pages to use for each change.
Yes, please send the exact steps. We plan to do this tomorrow morning.

Here are the recommended steps: 1) Go to Admin Settings > Workspace Profile to update the workspace name. 2) Go to Admin Settings > Email/Notifications to change the default sender name. 3) Go to Account > Contacts to update the primary account contact. 4) Keep at least one other full admin active during the change window. 5) After saving, run a test ticket, confirm the new sender display, and review one standard report to verify naming appears as expected. If anything looks off during the change, reply here and we can review before escalating.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: SSO login loop after enforcing domain restriction
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: I turned on Google SSO and restricted access to users from our company domain yesterday. Since then, three teammates can log in, but everyone else gets sent back to the sign-in page after authenticating. I need to know whether I misconfigured something in our workspace settings or if this is a bug.

comments.message: We enabled Google SSO, checked 'require SSO for all users,' and added our domain. Now most users authenticate with Google and then land right back on the login screen. Admins can still get in.

This usually happens when the identity provider is sending an email domain that doesn't exactly match the allowed domain in your authentication settings. Please go to Admin Settings > Security > SSO and confirm the allowed domain list only includes your primary domain, without spaces. Also verify that affected users are assigned to an active team in your workspace, because unassigned users can be redirected back to login.

I found that our domain list had both acme.com and Acme.com entered separately from testing, and the affected users had not been provisioned into teams yet. After fixing both, two users can log in, but one still loops.

Good catch. For the remaining user, ask them to clear cookies for our app and retry, then check whether their Google account email matches the exact email in their user profile. If it still loops after that, send us the user's email and a HAR capture and I'll escalate it, but the workspace configuration issue appears to be mostly resolved.

subject: Credit card updated but overdue banner won't clear

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Overbilled for suspended users, Dropbox sync paused, and 2FA reset not working
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: We were charged this month for several users who had already been suspended, and around the same time our Dropbox integration stopped syncing attachments. I’m also locked out of my admin account because I changed phones and the 2FA reset link sends me to a page saying my session is invalid. Another admin can still get in, but they can’t see the billing details. Please help us regain access and figure out why suspended users are still being billed while integrations are failing.

comments.message: We were charged this month for several users who had already been suspended, and around the same time our Dropbox integration stopped syncing attachments. I’m also locked out of my admin account because I changed phones and the 2FA reset link sends me to a page saying my session is invalid. Another admin can still get in, but they can’t see the billing details. Please help us regain access and figure out why suspended users are still being billed while integrations are failing.

I reviewed the account and the suspended users are still billable because they were not fully deactivated before the invoice snapshot; they were only access-suspended. Your Dropbox integration paused because the app owner is your locked admin account, and when 2FA recovery was attempted multiple times, our security service invalidated the active sessions tied to that owner token. I’ve issued a manual 2FA recovery for your admin login, promoted the secondary admin to billing visibility temporarily, and transferred the Dropbox integration ownership so sync can resume.
I got back in, thanks. Dropbox started syncing again, but finance wants to know if the suspended users will be credited.

I can confirm the current invoice is still based on the snapshot taken before full deactivation, so the charge was expected by system rules, but I’ve submitted a billing exception request because the users were intentionally suspended and non-operational. I can’t guarantee approval, though the note includes the exact suspension dates and the fact that the access loss and integration interruption happened together.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Zmiana nazwy firmy po rebrandingu
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Nasza firma zmieniła nazwę i chciałbym zaktualizować nazwę organizacji widoczną w aplikacji, na fakturach oraz w stopce e-maili systemowych. W ustawieniach udało mi się zmienić tylko nazwę zespołu, ale stare dane dalej pojawiają się w kilku miejscach.

comments.message: Dzień dobry, zmieniliśmy nazwę z DataPoint sp. z o.o. na Vistaline sp. z o.o. W panelu zmieniłem nazwę workspace, ale faktury nadal są wystawiane na starą nazwę i klienci widzą starą nazwę nadawcy w e-mailach.

Dziękuję za zgłoszenie. To są trzy osobne ustawienia: nazwa workspace, dane rozliczeniowe i nazwa nadawcy wiadomości. Nazwę workspace może Pan zmienić samodzielnie, ale dane na fakturach wymagają aktualizacji w sekcji Rozliczenia, a nazwa nadawcy jest w Ustawienia > E-mail. Jeśli chce Pan, mogę opisać kroki dokładniej.
Poproszę o dokładne kroki, bo w sekcji Rozliczenia nie widzę pola do edycji nazwy prawnej firmy.

Jasne. Proszę przejść do Rozliczenia > Dane firmy > Edytuj profil rozliczeniowy. Jeśli pole nazwy prawnej jest zablokowane, oznacza to, że konto ma ręcznie ustawiony profil przez nasz zespół finansowy. W takim przypadku już przekazałem prośbę o zmianę na Vistaline sp. z o.o. Zmianę nazwy nadawcy może Pan zrobić sam od razu, a aktualizacja danych fakturowych powinna być widoczna na kolejnej fakturze.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Merged two departments, changed account structure, and now charges are duplicated
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: We consolidated our Support and Success departments into one workspace and updated our ticket-routing automation so enterprise accounts get assigned to a new shared queue. After the account merge, I’m seeing duplicate subscription charges for both legacy departments, and the automation is still creating tasks under the old Support account owner. I need confirmation of which account is the billable parent now, whether the duplicate invoice will be voided, and how to make the automation use the new merged ownership model.

comments.message: There are two invoices this month—one for the old Support workspace and one for the merged workspace. We were told the merge would retire the old billing account.

Account merges don’t automatically cancel prior subscriptions unless the legacy workspace is explicitly closed. I can confirm the merged workspace is active, but the old Support workspace remains billable because it still has automation jobs running under it.
That’s likely because the routing workflow still references the old owner and queue. Can you shut off billing on the old workspace now?

I can’t terminate a subscription without authorization from the current billing admin listed on that legacy workspace, and that’s still your former Support director. I’ve temporarily paused the active automation jobs there to prevent further task creation, and I’ve escalated the duplicate charge review plus ownership transfer request to our billing team. For the new workflow, replace the destination workspace ID in the enterprise routing step with the merged workspace ID; right now it still posts to the retired environment.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Billing contact updated, tax exemption not carrying over
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: I took over as account owner from our previous operations lead and updated all the billing details, including our company address and tax ID. Since then, our newest invoice no longer reflects our tax-exempt status and includes sales tax. Can you make sure the account ownership change didn't wipe out our billing exemption and reissue the invoice?

comments.message: I took over as account owner from our previous operations lead and updated all the billing details, including our company address and tax ID. Since then, our newest invoice no longer reflects our tax-exempt status and includes sales tax. Can you make sure the account ownership change didn't wipe out our billing exemption and reissue the invoice?

When account details are changed, tax settings sometimes need to be revalidated. I can see your old exemption certificate expired last month, so the system charged tax on the latest invoice. If you upload the renewed certificate in Billing Settings, future invoices should be fine.
The certificate doesn't expire until December, and we already uploaded it last quarter. The issue started only after I changed the account owner and address.

Thanks for clarifying. I may be looking at the wrong document version from your file list. I've opened an internal review with our tax and billing team to verify whether the ownership change detached the exemption profile. Once confirmed, they can reissue the invoice if tax was applied incorrectly.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Charged for sandbox environment that was only enabled for testing
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: I enabled a sandbox last month to test a new ticket form and custom routing setup, but we only needed it for a few days. Our latest invoice includes a full month's Sandbox Environment charge. I deleted the test forms afterward, so I assumed the sandbox was off. Did I miss a separate configuration step, and can that fee be removed?

comments.message: The sandbox was used around June 3-5 for testing forms and assignment rules. We removed the test config, but the invoice dated June 30 still has the sandbox line item.

Deleting forms and routing rules inside the sandbox doesn't deactivate the sandbox subscription itself. That setting is managed in Billing, not in the configuration area where the test assets were created.
Okay, but I don't see a way to tell whether it's still active now. Can you verify it?

I can confirm the sandbox add-on is still marked active on your account. To prevent future charges, go to Account Settings > Environments and disable the sandbox, then remove the add-on from Billing > Manage Plan. I can't directly remove the past charge from support, so I've escalated your refund request to billing for review.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Charged for custom objects we only needed for a one-time import
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: We created a temporary custom object to import event attendance data for analysis, then deleted the object after exporting the results. Our bill now includes an add-on for custom object volume. I don't understand whether deleted imported data still affects billing, and I need advice on a better way to stage one-time data loads.

comments.message: We loaded around 450,000 attendance rows into a custom object for two days, ran our report, then removed the object entirely. Why are we being billed as if we still store that data?

Custom object billing is measured from the monthly high-water mark of stored records, not only what remains at invoice time. Because the import briefly pushed your account above the included volume, the add-on was triggered for that cycle. For one-time analysis, a safer option is to use the sandbox workspace or our external warehouse connector so the raw event data stays outside production.
Can we get that charge waived? Also, if we need to do this again, how do we avoid loading duplicate attendee rows from future CSVs?

I can't approve a waiver myself, but I've submitted a request to Billing noting that this was temporary analytical data. For future imports, set a unique key such as event_id plus attendee_email and choose upsert instead of insert-only. That will update existing rows rather than creating duplicates, which helps control both data quality and billable volume. If you'd like, I can also arrange a short education session with our data onboarding team.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: File attachments fail only for drag and drop
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Users on our account can upload attachments if they click the upload button, but dragging files into the comment box fails silently. No progress bar, no error, and nothing gets attached. This started after last week's release. We've tried PDFs and PNGs, small files and larger ones, and it's happening for multiple agents in Chrome. Since click-to-upload still works, it feels like something specific to drag and drop broke.

comments.message: Drag-and-drop attachments stopped working, although the normal upload button still works. How can we troubleshoot this?
Please open your browser console and check for any errors when you drag a file in. Also let me know whether any browser extensions are active that might interfere with the page.
We tested on two machines. Both showed a console error mentioning 'dropzone undefined' when dragging a file in, and one browser had no extensions at all.

That error is useful. It suggests a front-end regression in the drag-and-drop uploader rather than a file validation problem. I wasn't able to find a workaround besides using the upload button for now, but I've documented the console error and escalated this to the web app team as a likely bug introduced in the recent release.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: SCIM-provisioned users are inactive but still billed
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: We use SCIM to provision users from Okta. I disabled 14 people in Okta last week, and in your app they now show as inactive, but our billing page still counts them as paid seats. Since this touches both provisioning/admin settings and billing, I need a clear answer on what state actually stops charges.

comments.message: All 14 users are marked inactive in the People page and they were deprovisioned by SCIM on the 8th. Yet Billing says 214 seats for this month. Does SCIM deactivation not release paid licenses?

SCIM deactivation removes access, but billing depends on your subscription model. For your enterprise contract, inactive SCIM users still count against the purchased seat bundle until the bundle size is amended. In other words, the admin state is correct, but the billing quantity doesn’t shrink automatically.
Then how do we reduce the bundle? I don’t see any self-service option.

Enterprise seat reductions can’t be processed in-app. You’ll need to contact your account manager at least 30 days before renewal. I agree the distinction is confusing, and I’ve passed that product feedback along, but there isn’t a mid-term adjustment available from support.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Payment succeeded but SAML users blocked and Marketo sync paused
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Our renewal payment shows as successful on the card statement, but inside the app it still says the account is restricted. Since that banner appeared, our SAML users can’t get in and the Marketo integration has paused all sync activity. I also noticed the invoice total is higher than our contracted amount because it includes seats for users we deactivated before renewal. Can you check both the billing discrepancy and whether the restriction is causing the login and integration failures?

comments.message: Our renewal payment shows as successful on the card statement, but inside the app it still says the account is restricted. Since that banner appeared, our SAML users can’t get in and the Marketo integration has paused all sync activity. I also noticed the invoice total is higher than our contracted amount because it includes seats for users we deactivated before renewal. Can you check both the billing discrepancy and whether the restriction is causing the login and integration failures?

I found the payment, but it hasn’t been matched to the invoice yet because the charge settled under a different card descriptor than usual. The restriction flag is therefore still active, and yes, that can block SAML logins and pause Marketo sync jobs. I’ve asked billing ops to manually apply the payment and opened an invoice review for the disputed seat count. I can’t remove the restriction myself from this queue, but I’ve marked the case as urgent because it is affecting both authentication and integrations.
Do you have any ETA? Our campaign launches this afternoon and nobody from marketing can log in.

I don’t want to promise a timeline I can’t guarantee. Billing ops is usually same day for payment matching, but urgent cases can still take several hours. As a temporary workaround, if you have any non-SAML local admin still active, they may be able to export the Marketo-bound data manually; otherwise we’ll need to wait for the restriction to be cleared by the billing team.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Charged after account ownership transition request
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: I asked our IT team to deactivate my predecessor's account and move admin ownership to me, but before that was completed, your system auto-renewed our add-on package and charged the card on file. We had intended to remove that add-on before renewal. Can someone both update the account owner and review the renewal charge?

comments.message: I'm the new finance manager at North Peak Labs. We need the account ownership transferred from Darren Cole to me because he left last month. We also meant to cancel the Analytics Pro add-on before renewal, but the renewal went through yesterday for $1,200. Since I still don't have full billing access, I couldn't make the change myself. Can you help reverse the charge?

I can help with both parts. I've sent a secure ownership transfer form to your email so we can replace Darren as the billing owner after domain verification. Regarding the Analytics Pro renewal, I can see the add-on renewed automatically at 9:14 AM UTC yesterday. Our policy allows cancellation within 24 hours of renewal for annual add-ons if there has been no usage since renewal.
Great, we have not used the add-on since renewal. Please cancel it and confirm whether the $1,200 will be refunded to the same card.

Thanks for confirming. I’ve submitted the cancellation and refund request within the eligible window, and billing has approved it. The add-on is now set to terminate immediately, and the $1,200 will be refunded to the original card within 5-10 business days. Once you complete the ownership transfer form, your billing permissions will be updated as well.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Account owner left company and we can't transfer ownership
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: The only billing owner on our workspace left the company unexpectedly, and their email account has already been deactivated. We can still log in as admins, but the ownership transfer page is locked because it requires confirmation from the current owner. We need to update the owner before our renewal next week. Please escalate if this needs a manual review.

comments.message: We've got a time-sensitive renewal coming up and no way to approve changes because the former owner is gone.

Thanks for explaining the situation. Ownership transfer without access to the current owner usually requires a manual verification process by our Account Protection team, since support can't bypass that control directly.
What do you need from us to move it forward?

I've escalated this to Account Protection and requested expedited review due to the upcoming renewal. Please reply with the company domain, the former owner's email, the proposed new owner, and either a recent invoice or your contract number so they can verify authority and complete the transfer.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Downgraded seats but still charged for previous count
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: I reduced our workspace from 18 seats to 11 last week because two contractors rolled off and some managers are sharing logins temporarily. The billing page now shows 11 seats, but the invoice generated this morning still charged us for 18. I also need the owner changed from our former operations lead to me since she left the company on Friday and I can't access some of the billing controls.

comments.message: I reduced our seat count on April 3, and the admin page reflects 11 seats now. Today's invoice still billed us for 18 seats. Also, our old owner, Melissa Grant, has left the company. Please transfer billing owner/admin rights to me at this email so I can manage the account going forward.

I checked the account and the seat reduction was made after your renewal threshold for this billing cycle, so the current invoice still reflects 18 seats. The 11-seat count should apply starting next invoice. For the owner change, I can update the billing contact, but changing the account owner requires confirmation from the current owner or a domain verification step.

Melissa no longer has access to her email, so she can't confirm anything. We use the same company domain and I can provide whatever proof you need. Also, if the reduced seats only apply next cycle, can you at least confirm whether we'll get any prorated credit?

Thanks. I’ve started a domain verification request to transfer owner permissions to you; you’ll receive a verification link shortly. On billing, because the decrease happened after the renewal processed, there isn’t an automatic proration for removed seats on annual-renewing monthly-billed plans. I’ve added a note for our billing team to review a possible courtesy credit, but I can’t promise one. Once verification completes, your billing admin access should update.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Pętla logowania w aplikacji mobilnej
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: W aplikacji mobilnej wpisuję poprawne dane, pojawia się na chwilę ekran główny i zaraz wracam z powrotem do ekranu logowania. Na komputerze działa normalnie. Problem występuje tylko na iPhonie.

comments.message: Mam pętlę logowania na iPhonie — po wpisaniu danych aplikacja na chwilę wpuszcza mnie do środka, a potem wylogowuje.
Czy próbował Pan odinstalować aplikację i zainstalować ją ponownie?
Tak, zrobiłem to dwa razy. W przeglądarce na laptopie mogę się zalogować bez problemu.
W takim razie to raczej problem po stronie telefonu. Proszę zaktualizować iOS do najnowszej wersji i spróbować jeszcze raz.
iOS jest aktualny. Nadal to samo.
Rozumiem. Na ten moment nie mam innych kroków. Proszę korzystać z wersji przeglądarkowej, dopóki aplikacja nie zacznie działać poprawnie.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Need urgent review of suspicious bulk export
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: I'm a workspace owner and noticed a very large customer export was triggered from our account at 2:13 AM UTC by a user who says they were asleep at the time. I revoked their session and reset their password, but I need help understanding what was exported and whether your security team can review the event. This may be a breach, so I don't want a generic help article.

comments.message: Please route this to whoever handles security incidents. I need audit details for the overnight export and confirmation whether any other suspicious activity occurred.

I understand the urgency. I can see the export event in your admin logs, but frontline support doesn't have access to the payload contents or the deeper forensic review tools needed for a possible account compromise.
What exactly happens next, and how quickly can someone respond?

I've escalated this to our Security Operations queue as a suspected account compromise and marked it high priority. They will review authentication events, IP history, and related admin actions, then contact you through this ticket. While they investigate, please keep MFA enforced for all admins and let me know if you'd like me to note any specific users or time ranges in the escalation.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Invoice says automation overage, but archived workflows are included
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: I’m trying to reconcile this month’s bill and the automation overage numbers don’t make sense. We archived three high-volume workflows halfway through the month, but the invoice appears to count runs from after the archive date, and one archived workflow even shows activity in the usage export. Please explain how workflow automation usage is billed after archival and correct the invoice if those runs shouldn’t be counted.

comments.message: Our invoice includes automation overage charges that seem to include archived workflows. One flow was archived on the 14th, but the export shows executions on the 19th and 20th.

Archived workflows should not accept new trigger events, but in-flight jobs and scheduled actions created before archival can still execute later and count toward usage. That said, they should appear in the logs as scheduled continuations rather than new runs. If you share the workflow IDs, I can compare them against billing usage records.
I sent the IDs in the attachment. At least one of them looks like a brand-new run, not a delayed action.

Thanks — I found one workflow where scheduled reminders explain most of the post-archive usage, but there are also 43 runs that look inconsistent with the archive timestamp. I’ve asked billing to place the overage dispute on hold while engineering verifies whether those executions were counted incorrectly.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Workflow notification emails stopped after payment method update
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: After we changed the company card in the billing section, our workflow automation that sends approval notification emails stopped delivering. The workflows still appear to run, but the email step is marked “suppressed due to account state” in the logs. We had a short payment failure before the card update, so I’m trying to understand whether billing status can suppress workflow actions selectively and what needs to be reset.

comments.message: We had a failed renewal payment yesterday, then updated the card and the invoice is now paid. Since then, our workflows run but every email action is suppressed due to account state.
That usually means the system still thinks your account is delinquent. Since your card is updated, please delete and recreate the email action in the workflow.
That seems extreme — it’s happening across eight workflows, and non-email actions still work fine.
I understand. I don’t see anything else from my side, so recreating the steps is the best workaround for now. If that fails, you may need to contact your email provider.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Auto-upgrade to annual invoicing after enabling advanced SLA settings
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: I was adjusting our SLA configuration and business hours, and around the same time we accepted an in-app prompt for 'better pricing.' Our latest invoice is now annual instead of monthly, which our finance team did not approve. I'm trying to understand whether changing those advanced support settings triggered a billing plan change or whether this was a separate subscription action.

comments.message: We enabled multi-policy SLA rules and custom schedules, then shortly after got a yearly invoice. The timing makes it look connected.

Advanced SLA settings are part of product configuration and wouldn't by themselves switch billing cadence. The annual invoice likely came from accepting a subscription quote in the billing prompt, though I understand why it looked tied to the feature setup.
No one on my team realized that prompt would finalize an annual commitment. Can we revert to monthly?

I can see an accepted annual order tied to an admin login on your account, but reversals for committed term changes need approval from our contracts team. I've escalated the case with the screenshot of the acceptance event and noted your dispute. Please don't pay the invoice until the review is complete if your finance process allows that.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Deleted admin account still appears as account owner
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: I removed a former employee from our instance two weeks ago, but her profile still shows as the account owner in the billing and security settings pages. I can’t transfer ownership because the dropdown errors out and says the user is not active. I’m a full admin and have already cleared browser cache, tried an incognito window, and confirmed she is fully deactivated under Users. This is blocking our audit review.

comments.message: A deleted admin still appears as account owner, and I can’t transfer ownership because the system says the user is not active.

You should be able to change account owner from Settings > Billing by selecting another active admin. If the previous owner is deactivated, just reactivate her briefly, transfer ownership, and deactivate her again.
That’s the problem: I can’t reactivate her because HR requires the account to remain disabled, and the transfer dropdown itself errors out before I can select anyone else.
In that case, please export your user list and re-import the admin users so the ownership field refreshes. That normally clears stale user data.

subject: Bulk API updates are bypassing a scheduled reminder workflow

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Teams calendar integration connected, but meeting links aren't appearing in reminders
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: I connected Microsoft Teams so our customer success managers can insert meeting links into automated reminders. The integration status says connected, yet reminders still go out without Teams join URLs. I need help troubleshooting the setup and understanding whether there is another feature we need to enable for this to work.

comments.message: The Teams integration is green, but our reminder emails still say "meeting link unavailable." We expected the Teams URL to be inserted automatically when a success plan review is scheduled.

A successful Microsoft Teams connection only authorizes the tenant; it does not automatically attach meeting links to reminder templates. You'll also need to enable calendar event enrichment under Automations > Meeting Settings and choose Teams as the default conferencing provider. After that, reminders only include a join URL if the underlying event was created or updated after the integration was enabled.
We turned that on and new bookings now work, but the reminders for meetings already on the calendar are still blank. Is there a way to backfill them?

Existing events keep the metadata they had at the time they were created, so they won't backfill automatically. The workaround is to resave those meetings from the scheduler or update them via the calendar sync so our system fetches the Teams join data again. If you have many affected events, I can escalate a bulk re-sync request to our integrations team.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Need a cleanup plan for old opportunities before training the sales team
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: We have thousands of stale opportunities that were never closed properly, and before I retrain the sales team I want a cleanup plan that won’t destroy historical reporting. I’d like guidance on what to bulk close, what to archive, and how to explain the rules to reps so we don’t end up back in the same state.

comments.message: We have thousands of stale opportunities that were never closed properly, and before I retrain the sales team I want a cleanup plan that won’t destroy historical reporting. I’d like guidance on what to bulk close, what to archive, and how to explain the rules to reps so we don’t end up back in the same state.

That’s a good use case for a staged cleanup. First, segment stale opportunities by age, stage, and last activity date. In most cases, deals that are clearly dead should be bulk moved to Closed Lost with a standardized loss reason rather than deleted, so they remain in reporting. Archive only if your workspace has a separate archival state for records you truly don’t want in active views. For rep training, define clear exit criteria for every pipeline stage and a rule like: no opportunity can sit untouched for more than 30 days without next steps or closure.
We have some opportunities from a migration that were never real deals at all. Should those still be marked Closed Lost?

Not necessarily. If they were placeholder records from migration and not actual pipeline, I’d separate them with a source flag and handle them in a one-time cleanup so they don’t contaminate sales-loss reporting. If your archive option excludes them from active workflows while preserving auditability, that’s usually better. If you want, I can also outline a sample policy your managers can send to reps.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Los tickets no llegan a Salesforce desde su integración
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Desde esta mañana los tickets cerrados ya no se están creando como casos en Salesforce. La integración estuvo funcionando por meses sin problemas. Revisé las credenciales y aparentemente siguen activas, pero en el registro solo veo un mensaje genérico de fallo de API. ¿Me pueden orientar?

comments.message: También noté que el problema empezó justo después de que un administrador cambió permisos en Salesforce, aunque no sabemos si está relacionado.

Entiendo. Si las credenciales siguen activas, probablemente no sea un problema de autenticación. Le recomiendo desconectar y volver a conectar Salesforce para refrescar la sesión. Normalmente eso resuelve los fallos de API.
Ya lo hicimos dos veces y sigue igual. Además ahora la integración muestra menos objetos disponibles que antes.
En ese caso podría ser una incidencia temporal de Salesforce. Les sugiero esperar unas horas y probar de nuevo más tarde.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Lead routing rule ignores region override field
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: I'm configuring lead assignment so that inbound leads normally route by country, but sales ops wants a manual Region Override field to take priority when it's filled in. I added the override field and updated the automation, but leads are still being assigned by country even when Region Override has a value. I need to know whether the field is not available to the workflow yet or whether the routing order is wrong.

comments.message: Example: a lead from Canada with Region Override set to EMEA still goes to the North America queue. The rule order is Override first, then country mapping. The field is a dropdown we added yesterday.

Thanks. New fields can take a few minutes to propagate to workflow evaluation, but if this has been longer than that, the more likely issue is the field value format. In Product Configuration, can you confirm whether Region Override stores `EMEA` as text or as an internal option ID? In the routing workflow, the first condition should be "Region Override is not empty," and then route based on the specific stored value.
It has definitely propagated by now. The stored values are plain text. I already have "is not empty" plus a nested check for EMEA.

In that case, try moving the country rule set into an else block under the override check. If both rule groups are top-level and your assignment action doesn't stop further processing, the later country mapping can overwrite the queue selected by the override branch. Publish the workflow again and test with the lead activity log open; you should see only the override branch execute.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: La integración con Slack no crea canales para nuevos proyectos
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Tenemos activada la integración con Slack para que cada proyecto nuevo cree su canal automáticamente, pero desde el lunes ya no se están creando. Los proyectos sí se crean bien en su sistema y el bot sigue instalado en Slack. ¿Qué deberíamos revisar?

comments.message: Además, los proyectos creados manualmente en Slack sí funcionan, solo falla la creación automática desde su plataforma.

Gracias. Cuando la creación automática falla pero el bot sigue instalado, normalmente revisamos permisos del bot y el log de automatizaciones. ¿Puede entrar en Integraciones > Slack > Registro y decirme si aparece algún error como "missing_scope" o "channel_name_invalid"?
Sí, veo varios errores "missing_scope: channels:manage" desde el lunes.

Eso explica el fallo. Slack revocó o cambió el alcance necesario para crear canales. Deben volver a autorizar la integración aceptando el permiso channels:manage. Después de eso, los proyectos nuevos deberían crear canal otra vez. Los proyectos ya creados durante la falla no se recuperarán solos, así que tendrán que ejecutar la acción "Crear canal" manualmente o desde una automatización en lote.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Necesito fusionar cuentas cliente sin perder historial
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Tenemos dos cuentas del mismo cliente creadas por error: una con el dominio antiguo y otra con el nuevo. Queremos fusionarlas porque los usuarios y oportunidades están repartidos entre ambas, pero no queremos perder el historial de actividad ni los permisos por equipo. No encuentro una opción clara para combinar cuentas padre con sus registros relacionados.

comments.message: Tenemos dos cuentas del mismo cliente creadas por error: una con el dominio antiguo y otra con el nuevo. Queremos fusionarlas porque los usuarios y oportunidades están repartidos entre ambas, pero no queremos perder el historial de actividad ni los permisos por equipo. No encuentro una opción clara para combinar cuentas padre con sus registros relacionados.

Sí, le ayudo. Desde la vista de cuentas puede usar la opción "Fusionar" si ambas cuentas están en el mismo workspace. El sistema conserva la cuenta principal, mueve el historial de actividades, notas y oportunidades, y luego desactiva la secundaria. Los permisos por equipo no siempre se heredan automáticamente, así que después de la fusión conviene revisar la pestaña de acceso.
No veo el botón "Fusionar" en ninguna de las dos cuentas. Soy administrador del workspace.

Gracias. En algunos planes esa función solo aparece si está habilitado el módulo de deduplicación avanzada. Revise en Configuración > Gestión de datos > Detección de duplicados. Si no aparece, puedo escalar una solicitud para que soporte técnico haga la fusión asistida desde backend. Envíeme los IDs de ambas cuentas y cuál debe quedar como principal.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Duplicate charges tied to duplicated workflow executions
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: I noticed we were billed for two overage blocks this month, and the usage report lines up with a workflow automation bug we’ve been seeing. Our lead assignment workflow has been firing twice for a subset of records, which appears to have doubled both task creation and billable automation runs. I need help separating a billing problem from an automation defect, because we shouldn’t be paying overages for duplicate executions caused by the platform.

comments.message: We got charged for 18,000 automation runs, but our normal volume is closer to 9,000. Looking at the logs, a lead routing workflow has been executing twice on the same trigger for hundreds of records.

I checked your invoice and the overage charges were calculated from recorded workflow runs, so the billing itself is mathematically correct. For the duplicate executions, can you confirm whether the workflow has more than one trigger or if records are being updated by another automation?
It has one trigger: when lifecycle stage changes to MQL. We do have a follow-up workflow that updates owner fields, but that shouldn’t retrigger the original path.

That follow-up update may be causing the original workflow to run again if re-enrollment is enabled. I recommend disabling re-enrollment and monitoring usage for 24 hours. I’m also opening a ticket with our technical team to review whether duplicate runs from the same event should be credited back to billing if they were system-generated.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Need to rename primary admin without breaking SSO provisioning API
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: I need to change our primary admin from a former employee to me, but I'm worried because our Okta provisioning flow and internal scripts still use the current admin's API client. Can support confirm what changes automatically and what needs to be migrated manually? If possible, please help coordinate because we cannot risk downtime.

comments.message: Our former operations lead is still listed as the primary admin, and we want to switch that to my account today. We also use the SCIM and user management APIs through a client he originally created. Will changing the primary admin break those integrations?
Changing the primary admin should not affect your APIs. The integrations are workspace-based, so you can go ahead and make the account change from the Admin page.
We already did, and now our SCIM calls to create users are failing with 403, while some older scripts still work. So it definitely changed something.

Thanks for testing that. I looked deeper and the SCIM client in your tenant is actually owned by the deactivated admin, so my earlier guidance was incomplete. Older scripts are working because they use a separate personal token. I'm escalating this to our identity team to transfer the SCIM app ownership and preserve the existing client ID if possible. In the meantime, please avoid deleting the former admin record completely.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: API token creator can no longer log in
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Our integration broke because the user who created the API token can no longer log in to your platform. He gets an 'account suspended' message even though HR says he is still active, and now we can’t rotate the token or inspect the integration settings. Please escalate this because it’s blocking production data syncs.

comments.message: The user’s password was reset yesterday after suspicious activity, and today the login says the account is suspended pending review. We need access to the account or another way to manage the integration immediately.

An account suspension message means our trust system placed a hold on the login. I can’t remove that from support, and for security reasons I also can’t expose or transfer the existing API token. I’m escalating this to our trust and safety team to review the suspension and to our integrations team to advise whether the token can be re-owned or replaced safely.
Is there any temporary workaround? The sync failure is backing up orders.

If another admin can still access the integration settings, they may be able to create a new token and update the downstream system, but that depends on your role configuration. I’ve added the production impact to the escalation so the teams can prioritize the review. Until they respond, I can’t restore the login from this channel.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Confused about archived projects versus deleted projects
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: I'm putting together an internal FAQ for our project leads, and people keep asking whether archiving a project removes reports, notifications, and API access the same way deleting does. The help center explanations are too short, and I don't want to give the wrong guidance. Could someone break down the practical difference in a way I can reuse with our users?

comments.message: I'm putting together an internal FAQ for our project leads, and people keep asking whether archiving a project removes reports, notifications, and API access the same way deleting does. The help center explanations are too short, and I don't want to give the wrong guidance. Could someone break down the practical difference in a way I can reuse with our users?

Yes — archiving is meant to preserve historical data while taking the project out of active workflows, whereas deleting is intended to permanently remove the project and its associated records after any retention window. In practical terms, archived projects usually remain available in reporting and audit history, but they stop appearing in normal active lists and may stop sending routine notifications. Deleted projects are removed more broadly and shouldn't be relied on for reporting continuity.
That's the kind of explanation I needed. One more thing: will archived projects still be accessible through the API for read-only reporting?

In most accounts, yes, archived items remain queryable for reporting-oriented API calls if the endpoint supports archived filters, but write actions are typically restricted. Since some endpoints handle archived records differently, I've escalated a quick confirmation request to our platform team and they'll follow up if your tenant has any exceptions. For your FAQ, it's safe to say archive preserves history; delete is for removal.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: La integración con Jira crea incidencias, pero en el proyecto equivocado
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: Configuré Jira para que los bugs reportados desde nuestro portal se creen automáticamente, pero todos terminan en el proyecto interno de TI en vez del proyecto de Producto. Ya revisé la autenticación y el tipo de incidencia, aunque no encuentro dónde se fija el proyecto por defecto ni si una automatización del producto lo está sobrescribiendo.

comments.message: En la pantalla de integración elegí el proyecto PROD y guardé. Sin embargo, cada ticket nuevo aparece en ITOPS.
Es posible que haya una regla de enrutamiento a nivel de formulario. Revise la configuración del formulario desde el que salen esos bugs y vea si tiene un proyecto de Jira asignado manualmente.
Sí había una regla en el formulario, pero incluso después de cambiarla algunos tickets siguen yendo a ITOPS.

Entonces revise también Configuración > Flujos de trabajo > Automatizaciones, porque una acción posterior puede estar reasignando el destino según la categoría o el equipo. Si no encuentra nada, exporte un ejemplo con la carga útil del webhook y compárelo con la configuración del proyecto por defecto. A primera vista parece una combinación de configuración duplicada entre formulario e integración más que un fallo de conexión.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Webhook test succeeds but live events never arrive
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: I'm setting up a webhook to our internal endpoint. The built-in test payload reaches us successfully, but actual live events from ticket updates never show up. I've been troubleshooting our firewall and don't see anything blocked, so I need help figuring out why test delivery works but production events do not.

comments.message: I'm setting up a webhook to our internal endpoint. The built-in test payload reaches us successfully, but actual live events from ticket updates never show up. I've been troubleshooting our firewall and don't see anything blocked, so I need help figuring out why test delivery works but production events do not.

A successful test means the endpoint is reachable, so the next place to check is whether the webhook is actually subscribed to the event types you expect and whether the rule is enabled for the right workspace objects. Please open the webhook configuration and confirm ticket.updated is selected, not just test or ticket.created. Also check whether any filter condition limits it to a specific queue or status.
You found it — it was only subscribed to ticket.created, and we were expecting updates and comments. I changed it to include ticket.updated and ticket.commented.

Great, that would explain the mismatch between test success and missing live traffic. You should now receive future matching events, but events missed while the subscription was incomplete will not be replayed automatically. If you want, trigger a small ticket update now to verify end-to-end delivery.

best_agent_prior_tickets.subject: Training request for automation rules, but docs don't match our screen
best_agent_prior_tickets.description: I'm trying to teach our support leads how to use automation rules, and the knowledge base article shows options that aren't visible in our admin panel. I need a clear explanation I can pass on to the team, because right now they don't understand the difference between trigger conditions, schedule-based automations, and one-time bulk actions. Can you clarify this in plain language?

comments.message: I'm trying to teach our support leads how to use automation rules, and the knowledge base article shows options that aren't visible in our admin panel. I need a clear explanation I can pass on to the team, because right now they don't understand the difference between trigger conditions, schedule-based automations, and one-time bulk actions. Can you clarify this in plain language?

Sure — triggers run immediately when a record matches conditions, automations run on a time schedule or after a delay, and bulk actions are manual one-off updates you launch yourself. If the article doesn't match your screen, it's probably because you're on an older plan or a different admin experience.
That explanation is useful, but I still need to know why our admins don't see the 'Run test' option from the article. We're preparing a training session tomorrow.

The 'Run test' option should normally be there under automations, so you may want to clear cache or try another browser. If it's still missing, I'll need to escalate to our documentation team because the article may be outdated for your account version.
